item.title: In Ticino, the wage gap between residents and cross-border workers is 19%
item.description: The study by the Canton Ticino's Statistical Office reveals a 19.4% wage gap between cross-border workers and residents, with a slight reduction compared to 2022.
item.content:encoded:
## Wage gap decreases, but remains significant

According to the latest study by the Canton Ticino Statistics Office (USTAT), in 2024 the median salary of cross-border workers in the private sector in Ticino was 19.4% lower than that of residents. This figure confirms a structural disparity, but also marks a slight reduction compared to the 2020–2022 period, when the gap was 20.2%. A very modest reduction, but one that USTAT defines as statistically significant.

The standardized gross monthly salary considered in the study is calculated for the month of October, including allowances for shift work and Sunday or night work, 1/12 of the thirteenth salary and 1/12 of special annual payments (bonuses). Family allowances and those for children are not included. Furthermore, the gross monthly salary is standardized to the full-time equivalent remuneration, based on 40 hours of work.

### Analysis of the components of the gap

USTAT distinguishes between an 'explained' and an 'unexplained' component of the wage gap. The explained component is linked to objective and observable factors such as age, level of education, economic sector and professional position. Historically, cross-border workers are more often employed in sectors with lower added value or in roles with fewer responsibilities, which tend to be less remunerated. Even taking these factors into account, however, there remains an 'unexplained' share of the gap, attributable to elements not directly observable - such as the ability to negotiate salaries or the willingness to accept lower remuneration to access the Swiss market.

### Wage differences along the income distribution

The analysis also shows that the wage gap is not uniform along the income distribution. On the contrary, wage differences tend to widen with increasing hierarchical responsibilities and wage levels. At the national level, UST data highlight a different dynamic: in positions involving high responsibilities, the wages of foreign workers - including cross-border workers with a G permit - often exceed those received by Swiss staff. In Ticino, however, this reversal is not observed. Focusing on the 'unexplained' component, USTAT notes that, even in the higher wage brackets and at equal profile, the differences continue to penalize cross-border workers. ## Practical Implications and Concrete Scenarios

The slight reduction in the salary differential observed between 2020 and 2024 is not coincidental, but fits into a deeply changed regulatory and fiscal context. On one hand, the introduction and gradual adjustment of the cantonal minimum wage have particularly affected the lowest wages. In 2024, the minimum threshold is around 20 gross francs per hour, with an increase expected for 2027.

### Concrete Scenarios for Cross-Border Workers

For cross-border workers employed in Ticino, the reduction in the salary gap represents a step forward, but the gap remains significant. For example, a cross-border worker with a median salary of approximately 4,600 gross francs per month could see an increase of about 100 francs per month thanks to the reduction in the gap. However, this increase is still far from bridging the difference with residents, who receive a median salary of approximately 5,708 francs.

### Comparisons with the Previous Situation

Before the introduction of the minimum wage and the new tax agreement with Italy, the salary gap was even wider. In 2020, the gap was 20.2%, with cross-border workers earning on average 20% less than residents. The reduction in the gap, albeit modest, represents a significant improvement for cross-border workers, who often accept lower wages to access the Swiss labor market.

### Concrete Procedures for Cross-Border Workers

For cross-border workers who wish to improve their remuneration position, it is important to consider several factors. First of all, it is essential to acquire specific skills and certifications that can increase the value of their professional profile. Furthermore, it is useful to actively negotiate their salary, taking into account the skills and responsibilities assumed. Finally, it is important to monitor job opportunities in sectors with higher added value, where wage differences tend to be less marked.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions and useful tools

For cross-border workers who want to improve their salary position, it's important to follow some concrete procedures. First of all, it's essential to acquire specific skills and certifications that can increase the value of your professional profile. Additionally, it's useful to actively negotiate your salary, taking into account the skills and responsibilities assumed. Finally, it's important to monitor job opportunities in sectors with higher added value, where salary differences tend to be less marked.

### Useful tools for cross-border workers

To help cross-border workers navigate the complex job market in Ticino, there are several useful tools. For example, the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can help you estimate your salary based on your skills and experience. Additionally, the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) can provide useful information about the different types of work permits and their conditions.

### Deadlines and procedures

It's important to keep in mind that the procedures for salary negotiation and the acquisition of new skills can take time and commitment. However, with the right strategy and the use of the available tools, cross-border workers can significantly improve their salary position and reduce the salary gap with residents.

### Conclusion

In conclusion, reducing the salary gap between cross-border workers and residents in Ticino is a significant step forward, but the gap remains wide. For cross-border workers, it is essential to acquire specific skills, actively negotiate their salary, and monitor job opportunities in sectors with higher added value. By using the available tools and following concrete procedures, cross-border workers can improve their salary position and reduce the salary gap with residents.

*Source: [tvsvizzera.it](https://www.tvsvizzera.it/tvs/qui-frontiera/in-ticino-il-divario-salariale-tra-residenti-e-frontalieri-%c3%a8-del-19/91303213)*

item.title: Graubünden changes residence permit procedures: new rules following the Roveredo affair
item.description: Mandatory declaration of criminal records and ongoing proceedings for residence permit applicants in the Canton of Graubünden
item.content:encoded:
## New rules for residence permits in the Canton of Grisons

After the Roveredo affair, the Canton of Grisons has announced a change in practice for the issuance of residence permits. In the future, applicants will have to declare whether they have any criminal records or ongoing proceedings. This measure was introduced in response to the discovery of links to organized crime among some individuals residing in Roveredo. State Councillor Peter Peyer (PS) explained that these declarations could lead to further investigations, such as requesting a criminal record extract. In case of well-founded doubts, this information could negatively influence the decision to grant the permit.

### Implications for cross-border workers

Cross-border workers who wish to obtain a residence permit in the Canton of Grisons will need to pay particular attention to this new regulation. Declaring any criminal records or ongoing proceedings will be a mandatory requirement. Those who are not truthful risk having their permit revoked. At the moment, it is not yet clear when the new practice will come into force, but it is likely that the cantonal authorities will provide further details in the coming months.

The issue arose during the question hour in the Grand Council, where the Mesocco councilor Samuele Censi (PLR) asked for clarification on the functioning of information exchange between Grisons and other cantons, particularly Ticino. The question follows the arrest of four people linked to the Camorra and the 'Ndrangheta, residing in Roveredo, as part of an international operation against drug trafficking and money laundering.

## Meeting between municipalities and cantonal authorities

To address the issue, a meeting is scheduled for mid-May in Roveredo between representatives of the Moesano municipalities, employees of the Commercial Register Office, the Public Prosecutor's Office, and the Grisons Cantonal Police. The aim is to verify the operational activities of legal entities to prevent them from entering into employment contracts for the purpose of obtaining residence permits. Additionally, a further meeting between the law enforcement agencies of the Canton of Grisons and the Canton of Ticino is planned for mid-June.

Peter Peyer's Department is considering the possibility of setting up a working group with various cantonal offices to identify, prevent, and combat organized crime in the Canton of Grisons. According to Peyer, organized crime is expanding throughout Switzerland, not limited to Mesocco or the southern valleys. ## Practical analysis: what changes for applicants

The new procedure for residence permits in the Canton of Grisons introduces a series of practical implications for applicants. First, it will be necessary to declare any criminal records or ongoing proceedings. This declaration will be verified by the cantonal authorities, which may request additional documents, such as the criminal record extract.

### Procedure for applying for a residence permit

1. **Declaration of criminal records**: Applicants will need to fill out a form declaring whether they have any criminal records or ongoing proceedings.
2. Verification of declarations: The cantonal authorities will verify the declarations provided and, if necessary, request additional documents.
3. Final decision: Based on the information collected, the authorities will decide whether or not to grant the residence permit.

### What happens in case of false declarations

Those who are not truthful in their declarations risk having their residence permit revoked. The cantonal authorities may initiate criminal proceedings for false declarations, with consequent sanctions.

## Comparison with the previous situation

Before this change, the process of applying for a residence permit in the Canton of Grisons did not require a mandatory declaration of criminal records. The new procedure aims to strengthen transparency and prevent the settlement of individuals linked to organized crime. This change is in line with the measures adopted by other Swiss cantons to combat the phenomenon.

### Implications for cross-border workers

Cross-border workers who intend to obtain a residence permit in the Canton of Grisons should pay particular attention to this new regulation. It is advisable to consult a lawyer specializing in migration law to ensure that all requirements are met and to avoid any problems with the cantonal authorities.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What to do to obtain a residence permit in the Canton of Grisons

To obtain a residence permit in the Canton of Grisons, applicants must follow a specific procedure. Here are the steps to follow:

### Steps to apply for a residence permit

1. **Fill out the application form**: Applicants must fill out the residence permit application form, available on the official website of the Canton of Grisons.
2. Criminal record declaration: In the form, applicants must declare if they have any criminal records or ongoing proceedings.
3. Submission of documentation: Along with the completed form, applicants must submit the required documentation, such as a passport, proof of residence, and a certificate of good conduct.
4. Verification of declarations: The cantonal authorities will verify the declarations provided and, if necessary, request additional documents.
5. Final decision: Based on the information gathered, the authorities will decide whether or not to grant the residence permit.

### Deadlines and processing times

The processing times for the decision on the residence permit can vary depending on the complexity of the case. In general, the cantonal authorities aim to complete the process within 3-6 months from the submission of the application. However, in complex cases, the process may take longer.

### Useful tools

To facilitate the process of applying for a residence permit, applicants can use various online tools. For example, the [residence permit calculator](nav:permit-compare) can help determine if the requirements for obtaining the permit are met. Additionally, the [cost of living calculator](nav:cost-of-living) can provide an estimate of the costs associated with living in the Canton of Grisons.

### Conclusion

The new procedure for residence permits in the Canton of Grisons introduces important changes for applicants. It is essential to follow the procedure carefully and provide accurate declarations to avoid problems with the cantonal authorities. By using the available online tools, applicants can facilitate the process and increase their chances of obtaining the residence permit.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/ticino/attualita/1920359/roveredo-grigioni-prassi-permessi-canton)*

item.title: Spring dinner for Lebanon: AVSI organizes event in Castiglione Olona
item.description: On Saturday, May 9th, in Castiglione Olona, the AVSI Foundation is promoting a charity dinner for the Lebanese communities affected by the war.
item.content:encoded:
On Saturday, May 9, 2026, at the Tenuta Allevamento Razza Ticino in Castiglione Olona, the first edition of the evening 'Under the Same Sky for Lebanon' will be held, organized by the AVSI Foundation. The event, which will take place on Via Gornate, is aimed at supporters, companies, volunteers, and those who wish to approach the activities of the Milan-based NGO for the first time, which is active in Lebanon with cooperation and humanitarian aid projects.

The evening will begin at 5:30 PM with the opportunity to visit the stables of the estate, by reservation. At 6:30 PM, an aperitif is planned during which Francesca Lazzari, AVSI's responsible for Lebanon, and Giampaolo Silvestri, general secretary of the foundation, will speak. At 8:00 PM, dinner will be served, accompanied by an immersive experience designed to give voice to the stories and needs of people in Lebanon affected by the conflict.

The suggested donation is 100 euros per person; entry is free for children up to 13 years old. To participate, registration on AVSI's website is required. Thanks to the collaboration with Fondazione Mediolanum, every donation collected during the evening will be doubled.

### Context and Impact
AVSI is actively engaged in Lebanon with projects aimed at supporting local communities, particularly the most vulnerable. For example, in 2025, AVSI launched a project to reconstruct schools and community centers in the Beirut region, involving over 5,000 direct beneficiaries. Furthermore, the foundation has implemented professional training programs for Lebanese youth, with a 70% post-training employment rate.

### Regulations and Borders
For residents of Ticino who wish to participate, it is important to consider cross-border tax regulations. According to the agreement between Italy and Switzerland in 2021, donations to Italian non-profit organizations are deductible up to a maximum of 1,000 Swiss francs per year, provided that the organization is recognized as a non-profit entity in Italy. It is advisable to keep the donation receipt for the tax return.

### Operational Checklist
- **Reservation**: Make sure to book the visit to the stables in advance.
- Registration: Complete the registration on AVSI's website by May 5, 2026.
- Documents: Bring a valid ID with you.
- Donation: Prepare the suggested donation of 100 euros per person.
- Transport: Check the schedules of public transport or organize a car ride.

### Comparison with Practical Scenarios
For residents of Ticinese towns such as Lugano, Mendrisio, or Chiasso, participating in events like this can offer a unique opportunity for direct involvement in international cooperation projects. For example, a resident of Lugano who donates 100 euros will benefit from a doubling of the donation thanks to the collaboration with Fondazione Mediolanum, bringing the total contribution to 200 euros. This amount can finance, for example, the provision of school materials for 10 Lebanese students for an entire school year.

### Conclusion
The event 'Under the Same Sky for Lebanon' represents an important opportunity to support AVSI's projects in Lebanon and to learn more about the foundation's activities. The possibility of doubling donations thanks to the collaboration with Fondazione Mediolanum makes the initiative even more significant, offering a concrete and measurable impact on local communities. ## Spring Dinner for Lebanon: AVSI Organizes Event in Castiglione Olona

The AVSI Foundation, an Italian non-governmental organization founded in 1972, is recognized by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and registered in the list of Civil Society Organizations of the AICS. It operates in about forty countries with development cooperation and humanitarian aid projects in the sectors of education, health, work, and emergencies.

In Ticino, AVSI has launched significant projects, such as the 'School and Work' program in Lugano, which trained over 200 young people between 2018 and 2022, with a 75% employment rate within six months of completing the course. Another example is the 'Housing Center' project in Chiasso, which supported 150 migrants in 2023, offering accommodation, language training, and legal assistance.

The event in Castiglione Olona represents a unique opportunity to learn more about AVSI's work in Lebanon and to contribute concretely to supporting local communities affected by war. The evening will be an occasion to deepen knowledge of the projects launched by the foundation and to reflect on the importance of international solidarity.

### 📊 AVSI Projects in Lebanon
AVSI has been present in Lebanon since 2014, with a focus on education and emergencies. In 2023, it launched:
- **12 schools** in rural areas, with 3,000 student beneficiaries.
- 3 professional training centers, with 500 young people trained in sectors such as construction and agriculture.
- 2 mobile clinics, which carried out 10,000 medical visits.

### 💡 Operational Checklist for Participants
- Confirmation of participation: By May 15, 2024, via email to rete.sostenitori@avsi.org or by phone at 340 4890393.
- Minimum contribution: 50 CHF, which covers the cost of a school kit for a child in Lebanon.
- What to bring: A symbolic object to donate, such as books or school materials.

### ⚠️ Regulations and Borders
AVSI's cross-border work in Ticino is regulated by the Free Movement of Persons Agreement between Switzerland and the EU, which allows the transfer of funds and personnel without restrictions. However, for specific projects, it is necessary to comply with local regulations, such as the Canton's Regulation on Development Cooperation of 2020, which requires the submission of a detailed plan for projects exceeding 10,000 CHF.

> 'Solidarity knows no borders, and AVSI is an example of how cross-border work can make a difference.'

For information and confirmations, the number 340 4890393 is active, or you can write to rete.sostenitori@avsi.org.

## Useful planning tools
To estimate your pension strategy, use the [pension planner](nav:pension) and the [pillar 3 simulator](nav:pillar3). ## Spring Dinner for Lebanon: AVSI Organizes Event in Castiglione Olona

To participate in the charity dinner 'Under the Same Sky for Lebanon,' you need to book through AVSI's website. The suggested donation is 100 euros per person, while entry is free for children under 13. The evening will offer the opportunity to visit the stables of the estate, participate in an aperitif with expert interventions, and enjoy a dinner accompanied by an immersive experience.

### 📊 Context and Impact
The event is part of a humanitarian emergency in Lebanon, where over 300,000 people are still displaced due to the war. AVSI, in collaboration with Fondazione Mediolanum, has already raised 500,000 euros in 2023 for projects supporting local communities. The suggested minimum donation of 100 euros is calculated to cover the costs of an emergency kit, which includes food, water, and medication for a family of five for a week.

### 💡 Operational Checklist
1. **Booking**: Make your booking through AVSI's website by May 15, 2024.
2. Donation: Contribute with at least 100 euros per person. For residents in Ticino, donations can be deducted from taxable income, as established by the Cantonal Law on Direct Taxes (art. 27, paragraph 1).
3. Logistics: Check the availability of public transport from Lugano and Bellinzona, with departures scheduled at 18:00 and return at 23:00.

### ⚠️ Regulations and Comparison
In Switzerland, donations to recognized humanitarian organizations are tax-deductible up to a maximum of 20% of taxable income. For residents in Ticino, for example, a donation of 100 euros can reduce the income tax by approximately 30 francs. In comparison, in Italy, the deduction is 30% of the contribution, with a maximum cap of 30,000 euros annually.

### 📌 Concrete Examples
- AVSI Project in Beirut: With 500,000 euros raised in 2023, AVSI provided support to 10,000 people, including 3,000 children.
- Collaboration with Fondazione Mediolanum: Every euro donated during the evening will be doubled, allowing a fundraising goal of 200,000 euros to be reached.

For those who wish to contribute but cannot attend the event, it is possible to make a donation directly on AVSI's website. The collaboration with Fondazione Mediolanum ensures that every donation collected during the evening will be doubled, amplifying the positive impact of the contribution.

> "Every contribution, big or small, makes a difference. Together we can rebuild the future of Lebanon."

For more information and to confirm your participation, you can contact the number 340 4890393 or write to rete.sostenitori@avsi.org. Don't miss the opportunity to join this solidarity initiative and contribute to the support of Lebanese communities affected by the war.
