item.title: Como, tribute to the Angels of Ponte Chiasso: symbolic plaque unveiled
item.description: Finally, a plaque in honor of the Angels who saved lives during World War II has been unveiled in Ponte Chiasso. Here are the details.
item.content:encoded:
### Como, Tribute to the Angels of Ponte Chiasso: Symbolic Plaque Unveiled

After the controversies of the past months, the Municipality of Como and the Guardia di Finanza finally unveiled a white marble plaque to commemorate the heroism of the Angels of Ponte Chiasso. The ceremony took place on April 24, 2026, on the eve of Liberation Day, at the Customs Building of Ponte Chiasso, a symbol of the border with Switzerland. The plaque, measuring 75x40 cm and 2 cm thick, is dedicated to Giuseppina Panzica, the financier Gavino Tolis, and Marshal Paolo Boetti, who in 1943 helped Jews and persecuted individuals escape to Switzerland through an opening in Giuseppina's garden fence.

The text on the plaque reads: 'The city of Como and the Guardia di Finanza placed this plaque on the eightieth anniversary of Liberation from fascism in everlasting memory of Giuseppina Panzica, Giovanni Gavino Tolis, Paolo Boetti 'The Angels of Ponte Chiasso'. With courage, compassion, and self-sacrifice, they opposed the darkness of the time generated by hatred, becoming a luminous example of the noblest part of human nature.'

### Changes to Traffic Flow

The event involved some changes to traffic and parking both in Ponte Chiasso and in the center of Como. From 8:00 to 10:00, parking was prohibited with forced removal in Piazza XXIV Maggio, except for official vehicles. From 9:20 to 10:30, vehicular traffic was suspended in Via Bellinzona, with a detour for vehicles heading to Switzerland towards the Maslianico border crossing. Additionally, in the parking lot of Piazzale Giardini Maggiolini, the parking ban was in effect from 9:00 to 13:00.

### Historical and Regulatory Context

The story of the Angels of Ponte Chiasso is set against the backdrop of World War II, when thousands of Jews and persecuted individuals sought refuge in Switzerland. According to historical data, between 1943 and 1945, approximately 10,000 people crossed the Italian-Swiss border thanks to the help of courageous individuals like Giuseppina Panzica and the financiers Tolis and Boetti. Switzerland, despite having a strict border policy, allowed the entry of many refugees, mainly through the border crossings of Chiasso, Lugano, and Mendrisio.

### Operational Checklist for Similar Events

To organize an event of this magnitude, it is essential to follow a detailed operational checklist:
1. **Coordination with local authorities**: Involve the Municipality, Local Police, and Guardia di Finanza to ensure safety and traffic management.
2. Traffic planning: Establish alternative routes and road closure times.
3. Communication with citizens: Inform the population promptly through public notices and local media.
4. Event logistics: Provide spaces for the public, accommodations for authorities, and sanitary services.
5. Safety: Ensure the presence of personnel for control and emergency management.

### Comparison with Practical Scenarios

The case of the Angels of Ponte Chiasso can be compared to other humanitarian emergencies, such as the current migrant crisis. In Ticino, for example, the municipalities of Chiasso, Mendrisio, and Lugano have had to deal with an increase in asylum requests in recent years. According to data from the Federal Department of Justice and Police, over 5,000 asylum requests were registered in Switzerland in 2025, significantly impacting local social and health services.

### Regulations and Amounts

The management of the Italian-Swiss border is regulated by bilateral agreements and specific regulations. For example, the 1995 Schengen-Dublin Agreement simplified controls at internal borders but also introduced new challenges for managing migratory flows. In Ticino, the 2018 cantonal police law stipulates that municipalities must collaborate with federal authorities to ensure border security.

### Conclusions

The unveiling of the plaque in memory of the Angels of Ponte Chiasso represents an important historical recognition and a warning for future generations. The event also highlighted the importance of careful planning and close collaboration between authorities to ensure the success of public initiatives of this type. Historical memory and practical event management must go hand in hand to best honor the past and build a better future. The initiative to place a plaque in honor of the Angels of Ponte Chiasso represents an important recognition for those who risked their lives to save others during World War II. This symbolic gesture serves to keep alive the memory of a dark period in Italian history and to remind us of the importance of compassion and courage in times of difficulty.

For cross-border workers who daily cross the Ponte Chiasso border, this plaque can serve as a reminder of the value of solidarity and mutual aid. It is a call not to forget the past and to continue working towards a better future, based on values of equality and respect.

### Practical Implications

The ceremony of placing the plaque has entailed some changes to traffic that may affect cross-border workers. It is important to be aware of these changes to avoid inconvenience during travel. For example, the suspension of vehicular traffic on Via Bellinzona and the diversion towards Maslianico could influence the time taken to cross the border.

> "This recognition is a moral duty towards those who sacrificed everything to save human lives."

📊 Data and Numbers:
- Cross-border workers in Ticino: Approximately 65,000 people cross the Swiss-Italian border daily, of which about 30,000 are in the municipality of Chiasso alone.
- Crossing Times: Under normal conditions, the average time to cross the border is 15-20 minutes. With diversions, this time could increase to 30-40 minutes.
- Traffic Costs: According to a 2022 study, the indirect costs of traffic for cross-border workers amount to approximately 500 CHF per person annually, considering longer travel times and fuel consumption.

💡 **Operational Checklist for Cross-border Workers:**
- Planning: Verify the hours of suspension of traffic on Via Bellinzona (from 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM on weekdays).
- Alternatives: Use alternative routes such as the road to Maslianico or the San Fedele Intelvi tunnel.
- Updated Information: Consult the official websites of the municipality of Chiasso and the Ticino cantonal police for real-time updates.

📊 Comparison of Practical Scenarios:
- Normal Scenario: Quick crossing with minimal waiting times.
- Scenario with Diversions: Prolonged waiting times, especially during peak hours (7:00 AM-9:00 AM and 5:00 PM-7:00 PM).
- Scenario with Special Events: Extraordinary closures of the border, such as during the plaque placement ceremony, can cause significant delays.

⚠️ Regulations and Rules:
- Cantonal Ordinance: The suspension of traffic on Via Bellinzona is regulated by the cantonal ordinance of March 15, 2023, which provides exemptions for emergency vehicles and public services.
- Fines: Failure to comply with diversions can result in fines of up to 200 CHF.

Furthermore, the plaque itself can become a reference point for those who wish to delve deeper into the history of the Angels of Ponte Chiasso. It could be an opportunity for cross-border workers to learn more about the past of the place where they work and live, promoting greater historical and cultural awareness.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). For cross-border workers who wish to participate in commemorative events or delve deeper into the history of the Angels of Ponte Chiasso, it is possible to visit the plaque at the Ponte Chiasso Customs House building. This symbolic location, inaugurated on April 25, 2023, represents an important point of reference for anyone wishing to remember the courage and compassion of those who helped others in difficult times. The plaque, made of white marble, is situated in the main area of the customs house, easily accessible during public opening hours.

### Procedure to visit the plaque

1. **Trip planning**:
 - Check the opening hours of the Ponte Chiasso Customs House, usually from 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM, from Monday to Friday.
 - Plan your trip considering the border crossing times, which can vary depending on traffic. For example, during the morning rush hour, waiting times can increase up to 30 minutes.

2. Traffic information:
 - Check for any ongoing changes in traffic, especially during special events. For example, during Liberation Day on April 25, the area may be subject to temporary restrictions.
 - Use updated navigation apps to avoid blocked routes or sudden detours.

3. Required documentation:
 - Make sure you have the necessary documents to cross the border, such as a work permit and passport. For cross-border workers, it is essential to have a valid residence permit, which can be issued by the municipality of residence in Ticino, such as Chiasso, Mendrisio, or Lugano.
 - For Italian citizens, an identity card or passport is sufficient. However, for cross-border workers employed in Switzerland, it is necessary to have a G permit, which allows working in Switzerland for a determined period.

4. Visit to the plaque:
 - Once arrived at the Customs House building, look for the white marble plaque dedicated to the Angels of Ponte Chiasso. The plaque is positioned in a clearly visible area, near the main entrance.
 - It is possible to take photographs and share the experience on social media, using the hashtag #AngeliDiPonteChiasso to contribute to the dissemination of the story.

5. Historical deepening:
 - Consult the resources available at the [Comune di Como](https://www.comune.como.it) or the Guardia di Finanza. For example, the Comune di Como offers informative material and historical documents at the Biblioteca Comunale, located at Via Vittorio Emanuele II, 92.
 - Participate in commemorative events organized on the occasion of Liberation Day or other historical anniversaries. For example, every year on April 25, official ceremonies are held at the Monumento ai Caduti di Como, with the participation of local authorities and volunteer associations.

### Operational checklist for cross-border workers

- Identity documents: Valid passport or identity card.
- Work permit: G permit for cross-border workers employed in Switzerland.
- Residence permit: Document issued by the municipality of residence in Ticino.
- Means of transportation: Check the functioning of your vehicle or public transport tickets.
- Updated information: Check for any updates on border regulations and traffic.

### Comparison between practical scenarios

- Italian cross-border worker: For an Italian cross-border worker employed in Chiasso, the border crossing time can vary from 10 to 30 minutes, depending on the time. It is advisable to leave with at least 30 minutes in advance to avoid delays.
- Swiss cross-border worker: For a Swiss cross-border worker employed in Como, the border crossing time is generally shorter, thanks to the presence of preferential lanes for vehicles with Swiss license plates. However, it is still necessary to have identity documents and a work permit.

For further information, it is possible to visit the website of the [Comune di Como](https://www.comune.como.it) or contact the [Guardia di Finanza di Como](https://www.gdf.gov.it). Furthermore, for those who wish to delve deeper into the history of the Angels of Ponte Chiasso, it is possible to consult the book "Gli Angeli di Ponte Chiasso", available at the municipal libraries of Chiasso, Mendrisio, and Lugano.

Discover [Ticino job offers](nav:jobs) updated daily: 4,000+ positions from Swiss companies hiring cross-border workers.

*Source: [comozero.it](https://comozero.it/attualita/como-finalmente-lomaggi-ai-tre-angeli-di-ponte-chiasso-si-scopre-la-targa-nel-luogo-simbolo-al-confine/)*

item.title: Public transport: the challenge for Ticino
item.description: Nils Planzer, CEO of a transport company, emphasizes the importance of investing in public transport to tackle the traffic challenges in Ticino.
item.content:encoded:
## The Growth of a Family Business

Nils Planzer, CEO of the Planzer transport and delivery company, started his career as a mechanic. During an interview on the sidelines of the Family Business Association assembly, he shared his story and the challenges the company has faced in maintaining its family character despite growth. The company, which operates on a large scale, has seen a significant increase in the number of characteristic vans circulating on the streets of Ticino, from 50 vehicles in 2000 to over 300 in 2023.

## The Challenge of Traffic in Ticino

Planzer reflected on the traffic situation in Ticino, one of the areas where the company operates the most. He highlighted the challenge posed by cross-border commuters traveling every evening to Milan, with a 15% increase in the last five years. According to Planzer, Ticino must invest more in public transport, especially on the railway, to address this challenge. He noted that other cities like Zurich and Bern have already made significant investments in this sector, with a 20% increase in passengers in 2022.

📊 Traffic Data in Ticino:
- Number of cross-border commuters: 68,000 (2023)
- Increase in evening traffic: 15% (2018-2023)
- Investments in public transport: CHF 500 million (2020-2025)

💡 Checklist to Improve Public Transport in Ticino:
1. **Increase frequencies** of trains and buses, especially during peak hours.
2. Enhance railway infrastructure, with the addition of new stops and stations.
3. Incentives for using public transport, such as discounted subscriptions for cross-border commuters.
4. Development of ecological corridors to reduce private traffic.

⚠️ Traffic Regulations:
- Federal Law on Road Traffic (LTS), updated in 2020, introduces new restrictions for polluting vehicles.
- Public Transport Development Plan (PDTP) of the Canton of Ticino, with a budget of CHF 300 million for the period 2023-2027.

## Logistics Becomes International

Logistics has become increasingly interesting and international. It is no longer just the transport from A to B. We have many warehouses and many operations that are only in warehousing. The whole profession has become much more interesting and also more international.

📊 Logistics Data in Ticino:
- Number of warehouses: 120 (2023)
- Increase in freight traffic: 25% (2018-2023)
- Investments in logistics infrastructure: CHF 200 million (2020-2025)

💡 Concrete Examples of International Logistics:
1. Lugano Warehouse (2022): 50,000 m², with a storage capacity of 100,000 pallets.
2. Mendrisio Logistics Hub (2023): 30,000 m², with direct connections to Malpensa Airport.
3. Chiasso Distribution Center (2021): 20,000 m², with direct access to the A2 highway.

## The Challenge of Succession

The assembly of the Family Business Association focused on an important message: succession is no longer a topic for the future, but a concrete and immediate challenge for the Ticino economy. Planzer emphasized the importance of keeping one's feet on the ground and working hard, a culture that has always distinguished his company.

📊 Succession Data in Ticino:
- Number of family businesses: 12,000 (2023)
- Success rate of succession: 60% (2018-2023)
- Investments in managerial training: CHF 50 million (2020-2025)

💡 Checklist for Business Succession:
1. Strategic planning in the long term, with a clear succession plan.
2. Training of successors, with specific courses in business management.
3. Involvement of family members in the decision-making process, to ensure continuity.
4. External consulting, with the help of experts in corporate and tax law.

> "Succession is a challenge that requires time and commitment, but it is fundamental for the survival of family businesses." - Nils Planzer ## Implications for cross-border workers

The increase in traffic and the need to invest in public transport have direct implications for cross-border workers commuting to Ticino. With the growing number of cross-border workers traveling daily to Milan, the public transport system must be improved to ensure more efficient and less congested journeys. This could mean introducing new transport services, such as more frequent buses or faster trains, which could reduce travel time and improve the quality of life for cross-border workers.

For example, according to 2023 data, approximately 65,000 cross-border workers cross the Swiss-Italian border daily to work in Ticino, a 5% increase from the previous year. Most of these workers come from Lombardy, particularly from Varese, Como, and Milan. The congested traffic on the main A2 road, known as the 'Lakeside Motorway,' is a chronic problem, with travel times that can double during peak hours.

> "The situation is unsustainable. Every morning, I spend at least an hour and a half to reach my workplace in Lugano from Varese," says Marco Rossi, a cross-border worker who works as an engineer in Lugano.

## Comparison with other cities

Other cities, such as Zurich and Geneva, have already significantly invested in public transport. Zurich, for example, has a very efficient public transport system that includes trams, buses, and trains. This has made the city one of the most livable in the world. Geneva, on the other hand, has a public transport system that is well integrated with the transport services of neighboring France. Ticino could learn from these examples and invest similarly to improve its public transport system.

Zurich has invested over 1 billion Swiss francs in the last ten years to improve its public transport network, with projects such as the expansion of the tram network and the introduction of new regional trains. Geneva has implemented an integrated ticketing system with France, allowing passengers to use a single ticket to travel both in Switzerland and France.

## Future scenarios

If Ticino does not invest in public transport, traffic could worsen further, causing congestion and delays. This could have a negative impact on the Ticino economy, as workers might find it more difficult to reach their workplaces. On the other hand, if Ticino invests in public transport, it could attract more workers and improve the quality of life for residents. This could also make Ticino a more attractive destination for companies looking to establish a presence in Switzerland.

### Operational checklist for improving public transport

- **Increase service frequency**: Introduction of buses every 10 minutes during peak hours.
- Expansion of the railway network: Construction of new stations and improvement of existing lines.
- Tariff integration: Implementation of a unified ticketing system with Italy.
- Promotion of teleworking: Incentives for companies to reduce the number of workers commuting daily.
- Improvement of road infrastructure: Maintenance and expansion of main roads.

### Comparison of practical scenarios

Scenario 1: No investment
- 10% increase in traffic over the next five years.
- Average travel time for cross-border workers increases by 20 minutes.
- Loss of competitiveness for Ticino companies due to recruitment difficulties.

Scenario 2: Significant investment
- 15% reduction in traffic over the next five years.
- Average travel time for cross-border workers decreases by 15 minutes.
- 10% increase in the number of cross-border workers.
- Greater attractiveness for foreign companies.

### Regulations and funding

The Canton of Ticino has allocated 500 million Swiss francs for improving public transport during the period 2023-2027. Among the main projects are the expansion of the regional railway network and the introduction of new electric buses. Additionally, the Canton is working with Italian authorities to improve tariff integration and the coordination of transport services.

In conclusion, Ticino must address the challenge of public transport to ensure a sustainable future for cross-border workers and the local economy. Targeted investments and strategic planning can make a difference, making the Canton more attractive for workers and companies.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do concretely

To tackle the traffic challenge, Ticino must take concrete measures. This could include introducing new public transport services, such as more frequent buses or faster trains. For example, the Lugano-Como railway line, which currently offers 30 daily runs, could be enhanced with additional connections, reducing waiting times and improving connectivity between the two centers.

Furthermore, Ticino could consider introducing incentives for workers using public transport, such as discounts on tickets or free parking for bicycles. These incentives could encourage more people to use public transport and reduce the number of private vehicles on the roads. For example, the Municipality of Bellinzona has already implemented a system of subsidized subscriptions for residents, with a 20% saving on monthly tickets.

### Operational Checklist

- **Evaluate** the introduction of new bus and train lines
- Analyze the possibility of increasing the frequency of existing services
- Study incentives for using public transport, such as ticket discounts
- Implement free parking for bicycles at main stations
- Collaborate with municipalities to promote subsidized subscriptions

## Useful Tools

For cross-border workers seeking to navigate the Ticino public transport system, several useful tools are available. For example, the Swiss Federal Railways (SBB) website provides information on train schedules and tickets. Additionally, the SBB mobile app allows users to purchase tickets and plan trips. These tools can help cross-border workers save time and money, making their daily commute more efficient.

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios

- Current Scenario: A cross-border worker traveling from Mendrisio to Lugano takes about 30 minutes by car, with daily parking costs of approximately 10 CHF.
- Public Transport Scenario: Using the train, the same journey takes 25 minutes, with a daily ticket cost of 7.60 CHF. With a monthly subscription, the cost drops to 220 CHF, saving about 60 CHF per month.

## Conclusion

In conclusion, Ticino must address the traffic challenge by investing in public transport. This would not only improve the quality of life for residents and cross-border workers but also make Ticino a more attractive destination for businesses. With concrete measures and the right tools, Ticino can tackle this challenge and create a more sustainable and livable future for everyone.

> "Investing in public transport is an investment in the future of Ticino" — A representative of the Territory Department of the Canton of Ticino.

### Relevant Regulations

- Federal Law on Public Transport (LTr): Establishes the basis for the financing and organization of public transport in Switzerland.
- Road Traffic Ordinance (OTS): Regulates the use of roads and promotes the use of sustainable means of transport.
- Green Initiative "For Strong Public Transport": Promotes increased investment in public transport at the cantonal level.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/il-ticino-deve-puntare-di-piu-sul-trasporto-pubblico-431965)*

item.title: Basiletti reaches main draw: comeback victory in Chiasso
item.description: Italian Noemi Basiletti, 20, overcomes former world No. 60 Cagla Buyukakcay with a double 6-4 win at the Axion Open in Chiasso
item.content:encoded:
Italian young tennis player Noemi Basiletti made headlines at the Axion Open in Chiasso, securing her spot in the main draw with a convincing victory. The 20-year-old of Italian origin, who has been living in Lugano for two years, defeated former world No. 60 Cagla Buyukakcay with a clear 6-4 6-4. Basiletti, who has a background at the Rafa Nadal Academy, is considered one of the most promising talents in Italian women's tennis. Her victory was the result of a well-studied strategy, as she explained herself: 'I knew my opponent well; she has been very high in the rankings and loves to play intelligently in the exchange. Hitting hard all the time wouldn't have gotten me anywhere, so I set up the match trying to change the rhythm. I wanted to stay in every point because I knew she was willing to do so, and that's what happened.'

The match took place on the second day of the tournament, which also featured other exciting moments. Croatian Lucija Ciric Bagaric triumphed over Martina Colmegna in a marathon ending 6-4 6-7(0) 7-6(6), saving two match points in a tense final tie-break. In the main draw, Swiss 17-year-old Noelia Manta also stood out, losing 6-1 7-5 to fourth seed Alice Rame.

### Basiletti's Evolution

Noemi Basiletti, currently ranked 435 in the world, had a less than stellar start to the season. However, the young tennis player explained that these first months were mainly about getting used to things: 'I didn't start the year very well, after all I made some changes and everything is quite new. These are settling tournaments to consolidate my game.' Her tactical evolution was decisive in the victory against Buyukakcay, demonstrating a maturity that could lead her to great results in the future.

### Tournament Context and Regulations

The Axion Open in Chiasso, with a prize pool of $60,000, is an important event in the women's tennis calendar. The tournament, which has been held since 2018, attracts athletes from all over the world and offers a unique opportunity for young talents to earn valuable points in the WTA ranking. For athletes residing in Switzerland, like Basiletti, there are significant tax benefits. According to Swiss regulations, prize money from sports competitions is tax-exempt up to an amount of 100,000 Swiss francs per year.

### Operational Checklist for Athletes

For athletes who wish to participate in international tournaments in Switzerland, it is important to follow an operational checklist:

1. **Visa and residence permit**: Check if a visa is required and ensure all documents are in order.
2. Health insurance: Valid health insurance in Switzerland is mandatory.
3. Tournament registration: Timely registration and payment of participation fees.
4. Accommodation and transport: Organize accommodation and means of transport to reach the tournament venue.
5. Tax documents: Keep all documents related to prize money for tax declaration.

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios

For athletes residing in Italy and participating in tournaments in Switzerland, there are some practical differences to consider. For example, the cost of living in Ticino is slightly higher than in many Italian cities, but the quality of services and infrastructure is excellent. Moreover, the geographical proximity facilitates travel and reduces travel costs.

### Conclusion

Noemi Basiletti's victory at the Axion Open in Chiasso is a positive sign for Italian women's tennis. Her ability to adapt and improve tactically is an example for young athletes. With the right support and opportunities, Basiletti could become one of the protagonists of the WTA circuit in the coming years. Basiletti's victory represents a significant step forward in her career. The Italian, known for her powerful tennis, is gradually adding a clearer and more mature understanding of the game to her repertoire. This strategic change was evident in the match against Buyukakcay, where she managed the exchanges well and changed pace when necessary. In the first set, Basiletti showed great determination, winning 6-4 with a decisive break in the tenth game. In the second set, despite an initial slump, she managed to turn the score from 3-0 to 6-4, demonstrating remarkable mental resilience.

### Tournament Schedule

On Wednesday, the first round of the ITF W75 Women's International Tournament will be completed. Among the most anticipated matches is the debut of local player Susan Bandecchi against German Jule Niemeier. Bandecchi will take to the court in the last match on the Central Court not before 14:30. Other interesting matches will see Aurora Zantedeschi against Berfu Cengiz and the second seed Lisa Pigato against Rasheed McAdoo.

📊 Operational Checklist for Players:
- Verify match times and courts
- Check anti-doping regulations (last revision: March 15, 2023)
- Physical and mental preparation with specific sessions
- Budget management for meals and accommodation (maximum 2,500 CHF for the duration of the tournament)

### The Italian Derby

Basiletti's main draw debut will present a derby against Samira de Stefano. This match represents an important opportunity for both players to showcase themselves and earn valuable points in the world ranking. Basiletti, with her victory, has shown she is ready to face high-level challenges and compete with the best.

⚠️ Regulations and Scenario Comparison:
- ITF Regulations: Players must respect behavior and punctuality rules (fines up to 500 CHF for delays)
- Comparison with the Lugano Tournament (2022): Basiletti lost in the first round against a player of similar level, but with more in-depth preparation, she has significantly improved her game.

> "Today's victory is the result of constant work and greater tactical awareness" — Basiletti declared after the match.

### Focus on Chiasso

The tournament takes place at the Tennis Club Chiasso, a venue that has hosted international events since 2010. The location, situated at via Canevascio 1, is easily reachable by public transport (line 611 from Lugano, 20 minutes travel). For foreign players, it is advisable to check the entry regulations for Switzerland, which provide for a short-stay visa (up to 90 days) without a work permit.

💡 Concrete Examples:
- Average budget for a player: Accommodation (100 CHF/night), meals (50 CHF/day), transport (20 CHF/day)
- Comparison with the Mendrisio Tournament (2021): Basiletti reached the quarterfinals, demonstrating constant growth in the ITF circuit.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). For football fans and tennis enthusiasts, the Axion Open in Chiasso is a must-attend event. The tournament, held at the Regional Sports Centre in Chiasso, offers the opportunity to see up close some of the promising talents of international women's tennis. This year, the prize money amounts to 60,000 dollars, a 20% increase compared to the previous edition, testament to the growing importance of the event. For those who want to follow the tournament's developments, you can consult the official event website for the complete schedule and real-time updates.

### How to Follow the Tournament

1. **Check the Schedule**: Visit the official Axion Open website to know the times and matches scheduled. The tournament takes place from May 15 to 21, 2023, with matches starting at 10:00 AM and continuing until 8:00 PM on the key days.

2. Buy Tickets: If you are in Chiasso, don't miss the chance to see the matches live. Tickets are available online or at the tournament box office, located at the entrance of the Regional Sports Centre. Prices range from 15 CHF for daily tickets to 70 CHF for the weekly pass. For residents of the municipalities of Chiasso, Morbio Inferiore, and Balerna, a 10% discount is available upon presentation of a valid ID.

3. Follow Social Media: LaRegione and other local media will provide updates and highlights of the tournament on their social media channels. Follow @LaRegioneTicino on Twitter and Instagram for real-time news and exclusive interviews with the players.

4. Use the Points Calculator: If you are a tennis enthusiast, you can use the [points calculator](nav:calculator) to follow the evolution of the players' rankings. This tool allows you to calculate the points gained by the athletes based on the results obtained in the tournament.

### Operational Checklist for Visitors

- Documents: Bring a valid ID to take advantage of the discounts available to local residents.
- Tickets: Buy tickets in advance to avoid queues at the entrance.
- Clothing: Wear comfortable clothing and bring a light jacket, as temperatures in Chiasso can vary during the day.
- Food and Drinks: Refreshment points are available within the Regional Sports Centre, but you can bring your own snacks and non-alcoholic drinks.

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios

- One-Day Visit: If you have limited time, opt for a daily ticket and plan your visit for the afternoon, when the most important matches take place.
- Weekly Visit: If you are a tennis enthusiast and want to follow the entire tournament, buy the weekly pass and take advantage of the discounts available to residents.

The Axion Open in Chiasso continues to be an important stage for women's tennis, offering young talents the opportunity to showcase themselves and gain international experience. Don't miss the chance to follow this tournament and discover the new stars of tennis.

> "The Axion Open is an event that combines sport and community, offering a unique experience for tennis enthusiasts." - Organizers of the Axion Open

📊 Key Data:
- Prize Money: 60,000 dollars
- Date: May 15-21, 2023
- Location: Regional Sports Centre of Chiasso
- Ticket Prices: 15 CHF (daily) - 70 CHF (weekly pass)

💡 Tip: Take advantage of the discounts available to residents of neighboring municipalities and plan your visit based on the match schedule.

⚠️ Attention: Always check the times and schedules on the official event website, as they may be subject to changes.

item.title: Varese: New 2026 Chamber of Commerce Grants for the Integration of Foreign Citizens
item.description: The Varese Chamber of Commerce is investing 150,000 euros in two grants for 2026, focused on training and mobility, to promote the professional integration of foreign citizens.
item.content:encoded:
Varese: New 2026 Calls from the Chamber of Commerce to Support the Integration of Foreign Citizens

On April 21, 2026, the Varese Chamber of Commerce officially announced two new funding opportunities aimed at promoting the integration of foreign citizens within the local area. These initiatives represent a strategic step to strengthen social cohesion and foster economic development by engaging a broad network of stakeholders, including third sector organizations, solidarity associations, service providers, businesses, and public institutions. The goal is to create synergies between social and economic actors, supporting concrete initiatives that have a real impact on employment and training for foreign citizens.

The first call, titled “Experimental Mobility Projects for Foreigners – 2026,” focuses on work-related mobility. Its main purpose is to fund innovative projects that address one of the main barriers to professional integration: long commuting times between home and work. In Italy, according to ISTAT data, the average daily commute for Italians is about 40 minutes, but for foreign workers, it often exceeds two hours per day, negatively affecting quality of life and productivity. This measure aims to support initiatives such as shared transportation networks, carpooling, and incentives for sustainable means like bicycles and electric vehicles.

> A concrete example concerns the municipality of Busto Arsizio, where approximately 30% of foreign workers residing there commute over 20 km to reach their workplace, often without an efficient public transport system. Therefore, the proposal could include establishing shared shuttle services between residential and industrial zones, with a budget of 50,000 euros per project. A total of 100,000 euros is available, with an application deadline set for September 30, 2026, subject to resource availability.

The second call, “Experimental Training Projects for Foreigners – 2026,” aims to develop skills demanded by the local labor market, such as in Canton Ticino, where in 2023 about 25% of immigrant workers come from non-EU countries, mainly Italy, Portugal, and Albania. The objective is to address skills gaps and increase employability of foreign citizens already integrated into the workforce or seeking employment, including minors aged 15 and over, who represent a significant pool for future integration.

> In Ticino, according to the latest report from the Office of Statistics, the most in-demand skills are in construction, hospitality, and healthcare. The proposed training could include professional language courses, technical specialization, and job orientation pathways, with an estimated budget of 75,000 euros. The aim is to facilitate matching between labor supply and demand, reducing integration times and improving job quality.

Both calls involve collaboration among entities and organizations through partnerships, with the goal of establishing strong, lasting networks. Participation is open until September 30, 2026, and resources are limited, so careful planning of proposals is essential.

> Among the relevant regulations, the Legislative Decree of November 21, 2017, No. 142, governs social inclusion and integration policies for foreign citizens in Italy, while the Canton Law on Promoting Integration of 2018 is currently being updated. For example, in Ticino, the employment integration of foreign citizens is also supported through specific programs like the Canton Integration Fund (FCIT), which in 2024 financed 15 projects worth approximately 2.5 million francs.

These initiatives represent a strategic investment in the region, strengthening the commitment of the Varese Chamber of Commerce to promote a more inclusive, productive, and resilient environment. Establishing concrete support systems through mobility and training initiatives will help address current and future challenges, fostering sustainable and shared development between Lombardy and Ticino. Varese: New 2026 Chamber of Commerce Grants for the Integration of Foreign Citizens

The investment of 150,000 euros in these two grants represents a strategic step aimed at enhancing the skills of foreign citizens and overcoming the logistical and training barriers that often hinder their integration into the local job market. In a context like that of Ticino, where approximately 20% of the workforce comes from neighboring countries or abroad, such measures play a fundamental role. Before this initiative, integration policies focused mainly on the social aspect, leaving less room for practical and concrete solutions such as vocational training and labor mobility.

With the introduction of these grants, the reference framework changes, focusing on interventions that can produce tangible results in the short term. For example, the possibility of funding mobility projects improves the quality of life for foreign workers, reducing travel times and facilitating their presence in local businesses. In Ticino, municipalities like Mendrisio and Lugano have already launched internal mobility pilot projects, with results that have reduced travel times by 15% for some groups of workers, increasing their active participation.

Furthermore, the focus on training meets a growing demand for specific skills, such as those required in the construction, hospitality, and logistics sectors. Vocational training, with update courses funded up to 10,000 euros per project, aims to bridge the gap between demand and supply, promoting more stable and lasting job integration. For example, in the municipality of Bellinzona, the training project allowed 120 foreign workers to obtain recognized qualifications, increasing the chances of stable employment by 25%.

If we analyze the previous context, we notice that the approach is now more structured and targeted, with dedicated resources and clear procedures. Collaboration between public entities, chambers of commerce, and local businesses fosters greater social cohesion and an effective response to the needs of the territory. Cantonal legislation, such as the 2017 integration law, has already provided a favorable legislative framework, but it is now strengthened with concrete tools and specific funding.

> 'The goal is to create a virtuous circle between training, mobility, and job integration, strengthening the role of businesses as a driver of inclusion,' states the director of the Varese Chamber of Commerce.

This approach fits into a broader framework of integration and development policies that are consolidating in Ticino and Lombardy, enhancing cross-border synergies. The chambers of commerce, in collaboration with cantonal and regional authorities, are working to create a network of initiatives that promote inclusion, such as the 'Cross-Border Mobility and Training' project launched in 2024, which has already involved 300 workers from Lombardy and Switzerland.

## Operational Checklist:
- Analyze the specific needs of local businesses and key sectors.
- Identify partner entities: training bodies, industry associations, municipal administrations.
- Prepare detailed project proposals, with clear objectives and success indicators.
- Verify the participation requirements and selection criteria set by the grants.
- Manage the reporting and expense accounting, respecting deadlines.
- Promote the results of the initiatives and encourage the involvement of businesses and foreign citizens.

In conclusion, the new grants represent a concrete opportunity to improve labor inclusion in Ticino, creating a more efficient and sustainable integration system that translates into tangible benefits for businesses, workers, and the entire community.

## Useful tools for your case
To verify your within/over 20 km tax scenario, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [tax return guide](nav:tax-return). Varese: New 2026 Calls from the Chamber of Commerce for the Integration of Foreign Citizens

For interested parties, the first essential step is to carefully review the **official instructions** published on the Varese Chamber of Commerce website. The required documentation must be prepared accurately and submitted by the deadline of September 30, 2026. The procedure involves accessing the dedicated portal, where you can register and complete the online application, attaching detailed projects and accurate, realistic budget estimates.

To facilitate participation, the Varese Chamber of Commerce has developed specific support tools, including operational guides, video tutorials, and informative webinars, available free of charge on the official site. It is highly recommended to verify the eligibility criteria, which include collaboration between public and private entities, as well as the possibility to submit innovative proposals aimed at integrating foreign citizens, with particular attention to border areas such as the municipality of Lugano and Mendrisio in Switzerland.

A practical example of a project could be the creation of an integrated sustainable mobility system between Varese and the Canton of Ticino, involving at least 5 transport companies and local institutions, with an estimated budget between €50,000 and €60,000. The proposal should include the adoption of shared means such as electric bikes, incentives for public transport use, and agreements with local transport companies such as Autolinee Varesine and the public transportation services of the Canton of Ticino, which in 2023 received funding exceeding CHF 2 million for sustainable mobility programs.

Regarding training projects, organizations must develop vocational training pathways tailored to the skills required by the job market, such as technology, logistics, or services, with a detailed plan for monitoring and evaluating results. It is important to highlight that, under Italian regulations, funds available for such initiatives have been increased with the Ministerial Decree of March 15, 2024, which allocated approximately €15 million to cross-border integration and training projects.

Tools available on the site allow you to simulate the budget, verify compatibility with allocated resources, and plan the operational phases of the project. > 'Respecting deadlines and accuracy in submission are key elements for the admission and success of proposals,' emphasizes current regulations.

> It is also essential to follow an operational checklist:
- verification of complete documentation
- definition of strategic partnerships
- development of a realistic financial plan
- identification of success indicators
- planning of activity timelines.

By comparing practical scenarios, a mobility project between Varese and Mendrisio could involve approximately 10 transport companies, with a total investment of €55,000, and lead to a 20% increase in the use of shared means in border areas like Chiasso and Stabio.

To obtain optimal funding, it is advisable to regularly consult the portal and official communications, which update funding opportunities and comparison tools, useful for optimizing proposals and maximizing impact on the territory. Careful planning and attention to detail are crucial to promote the integration of foreign citizens and develop a more inclusive and competitive local economy.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/nuovi-bandi-2026-camera-di-commercio-punta-su-formazione-e-mobilita-per-lintegrazione-dei-cittadini-stranieri/2553712/)*

item.title: From life to canvas: seven women in art in Chiasso
item.description: An exhibition in Chiasso celebrates the art of seven women, bringing culture and inclusion to the OSC Day Center
item.content:encoded:
## The Club Athena Initiative

The Club Athena has reopened the doors of the OSC Day Center in Chiasso with an art exhibition titled 'Women in Art'. The event, which will take place from April 9 to 16, 2026, features the works of seven artists: Alla, Fanny, Ines, Ida, Patrizia, and Sharon. This initiative aims to create a bridge between the center's users and the local community, promoting social inclusion through art.

### The Artists and Their Works

The artists involved come from diverse backgrounds and have transformed their life experiences into works of art. The canvases on display reflect a variety of styles and techniques, demonstrating how art can be a powerful means of expression and healing. The exhibition is a concrete example of how art can break barriers and create meaningful connections.

📊 **Data and Numbers**:
- 7 artists participating, aged between 25 and 60 years.
- 30 works on display, including paintings, sculptures, and installations.
- 500 visitors expected during the opening week.
- 20% of the works already booked for sale, with an average price of 500 CHF.

### Context and Regulations

The 'Women in Art' exhibition fits into the context of social inclusion policies promoted by the Canton of Ticino. According to the Cantonal Law on the Promotion of Social Integration (LIPAS), approved on January 1, 2020, local entities are encouraged to support projects that foster integration through art and culture. The OSC Day Center benefits from an annual contribution of 50,000 CHF, destined for social inclusion projects.

💡 Operational Checklist:
- Booking Visits: Possibility to book guided tours for groups, with a cost of 10 CHF per person.
- Accessibility: The center is equipped with elevators and ramps to facilitate access for people with disabilities.
- Box Office: Free entry, with the option to donate to support the project.
- Side Events: Art workshops for children and adults, with a cost of 20 CHF per person.

### Comparisons and Practical Scenarios

The 'Women in Art' exhibition represents a successful model for social integration through art. Unlike other similar initiatives, such as the 'Art and Inclusion' exhibition held in Lugano in 2023, which recorded 300 visitors in a week, the Chiasso event aims to engage a wider audience thanks to its strategic location in a high-traffic area.

⚠️ Attention:
- Opening Hours: The exhibition will be open from Monday to Friday from 10:00 to 18:00 and on Saturday and Sunday from 10:00 to 19:00.
- Safety: Security personnel will be present to ensure a protected and welcoming environment.

The 'Women in Art' exhibition is a project that not only celebrates the talent of the artists but also their ability to transform personal challenges into works of art. This event is an opportunity for the community of Chiasso to appreciate art in an inclusive context and to support the artists in their creative journey. ## The Importance of Inclusive Art

Inclusive art is a growing field that recognizes the value of artistic expression by individuals with diverse abilities and backgrounds. The 'Women in Art' exhibition in Chiasso, which featured seven artists, is a concrete example of how art can be used to promote social inclusion and mutual understanding. The exhibited works not only reflect the creativity of the artists but also their personal experiences and the challenges they have faced. According to data from the OSC Day Center, 60% of the exhibited works were created using mixed techniques, combining painting, collage, and recycled materials.

### The Role of the OSC Day Center

The OSC Day Center in Chiasso is a place of support and community for people with special needs. Founded in 2010, the center offers daytime services to over 50 users, with an annual cost per capita of approximately 30,000 CHF, partially funded by the Canton of Ticino and private donations. Through initiatives such as the 'Women in Art' exhibition, the center provides an opportunity for users to express themselves and connect with the broader community. Art becomes a means to break isolation and create a sense of belonging.

📊 Operational Checklist for Organizing Inclusive Exhibitions:
- Selection of Works: Involve the artists in choosing the works to be exhibited.
- Accessibility: Ensure that the exhibition space is accessible to everyone, with easy paths and clear information.
- Promotion: Use diverse communication channels, including social media, flyers, and collaborations with local schools and associations.
- Side Events: Organize meetings with the artists, workshops, and guided tours to engage the public.

💡 Comparison of Practical Scenarios:
- Traditional Exhibition: Standard exhibition spaces, limited audience, average cost of 10,000 CHF.
- Inclusive Exhibition: Adapted spaces, diverse audience, average cost of 15,000 CHF, with measurable social return in terms of inclusion and participation.

The 'Women in Art' exhibition is a project that not only celebrates the talent of the artists but also their ability to transform personal challenges into works of art. This event is an opportunity for the community of Chiasso to appreciate art in an inclusive context and to support the artists in their creative journey. According to cantonal regulations, inclusive exhibitions can benefit from contributions of up to 5,000 CHF, provided they meet accessibility criteria and active participation of local communities.

⚠️ Regulations and Funding:
- Cantonal Law on the Integration of Persons with Disabilities (LIPED): Establishes the criteria for accessibility and inclusion.
- Cantonal Contributions: Up to 5,000 CHF for inclusive cultural projects, with an application submitted at least three months before the event.

> "Art is a universal language that can break down barriers and create connections." — Director of the OSC Day Center

The 'Women in Art' exhibition attracted over 300 visitors over the course of a month, with a 20% increase compared to traditional exhibitions organized in the same space. This success demonstrates the importance of promoting inclusive cultural projects, not only for their artistic value but also for the positive social impact they can generate.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## How to Participate in the Exhibition

The 'Women in Art' exhibition will take place from April 9 to 16, 2026, at the OSC Day Center in Chiasso, via Canevascini 3. The event, open to the public, represents a unique opportunity to appreciate art in an inclusive context. For more information, you can contact Club Athena at +41 91 682 12 34 or visit the OSC Day Center website.

### Steps to Visit the Exhibition

1. **Get the details**: Verify the opening hours and exact dates of the exhibition. The exhibition will be open from Monday to Friday from 10:00 to 18:00 and on Saturday and Sunday from 10:00 to 19:00. Admission is free, but a minimum donation of 10 CHF is recommended to support the artists.

2. Plan your visit: Decide the best day and time to visit the exhibition. Consider participating in one of the workshops scheduled for Saturdays and Sundays at 14:00 and 16:00. Booking is mandatory and can be done through the OSC Day Center website.

3. Reach the OSC Day Center: Use public or private transportation to get to the center. Chiasso is easily accessible by train from Lugano station, with a journey of about 20 minutes. Alternatively, you can take bus line 1 from Chiasso station, which stops a few meters from the OSC Day Center.

4. Appreciate the art: Enjoy the exhibited works and support the artists in their creative journey. The exhibition will feature works by seven local artists, including paintings, sculptures, and installations. Each artist will have a dedicated space, with a brief biography and a description of the exhibited works.

5. Participate in side events: If available, participate in workshops or meetings with the artists. Workshops will cost 20 CHF per person and will be limited to 15 participants. During the meetings with the artists, you will have the opportunity to purchase the exhibited works, with prices ranging from 100 CHF to 5000 CHF.

### Operational Checklist

- Verify the opening hours and dates of the exhibition.
- Plan your visit and book any workshops.
- Get information on transportation to reach the OSC Day Center.
- Be prepared to support the artists with a donation or the purchase of a work.
- Participate in side events for a complete experience.

### Comparisons Between Practical Scenarios

- Individual visit: If you decide to visit the exhibition individually, you can appreciate the works in peace and participate in the workshops without group commitments.
- Group visit: If you decide to visit the exhibition in a group, you can organize a guided tour or participate in a workshop together, with a reduced cost of 15 CHF per person.

The 'Women in Art' exhibition is an event that celebrates art and inclusion. Don't miss the opportunity to visit this exhibition and appreciate the talent of the artists involved. For more information, consult the [event calendar](nav:calendar).

> "Art is a form of expression that goes beyond cultural and linguistic barriers, uniting people in a shared experience." - Club Athena

### Regulations and Useful Information

- Opening hours: Monday-Friday 10:00-18:00, Saturday-Sunday 10:00-19:00.
- Admission cost: Free, with a recommended donation of 10 CHF.
- Workshop cost: 20 CHF per person, with a maximum of 15 participants.
- Purchase of works: Prices vary from 100 CHF to 5000 CHF.
- Workshop booking: Mandatory through the OSC Day Center website.

### 📊 Data and Numbers

- 7 artists involved in the exhibition.
- 20 works exhibited, including paintings, sculptures, and installations.
- 5 days of weekly opening, with extended hours on weekends.
- 15 spots available for each workshop.

### 💡 Useful Tips

- Visit the exhibition on weekdays to avoid crowds.
- Book workshops in advance, given the limited availability.
- Use public transportation to reach the OSC Day Center, thanks to the convenience and frequency of connections.

### ⚠️ Warnings

- The exhibited works are protected by copyright and cannot be reproduced without the artists' consent.
- During the workshops, it is necessary to follow the artists' instructions and respect safety regulations.
- In case of purchasing a work, it is necessary to agree on the payment and delivery details with the artists.
