item.title: Health tax for cross-border workers: repeal rejected, but cuts are coming
item.description: The Lombardy Region has rejected the Pd's motion to abolish the health tax for cross-border workers, but is aiming for deductions and minimum rates.
item.content:encoded:
## The decision of the Regional Council

The Regional Council of Lombardy has rejected the Pd's motion calling for the abolition of the health tax for cross-border workers. The decision was made during a session in which the motion by Luigi Zocchi of Fratelli d'Italia was approved. The tax, which concerns the so-called 'old cross-border workers', will remain in force, but the Region will seek to soften it with deductions and tax discounts.

### The reasons for the decision

The health tax for cross-border workers was confirmed because it is a national law that cannot be ignored. However, the Lombardy Region has decided to negotiate with Rome to obtain the minimum tax burden and forms of lump-sum taxation for cross-border workers. The goal is to apply the tax to the lowest possible extent.

The tax will serve to finance the national health system and increase the salaries of the personnel of the border Asst. The local government official Massimo Sertori estimated increases of 10,000 euros per year for doctors and over 5,000 euros for nurses.

## Political reactions

From the majority, Silvana Snider of the Lega defended the principle that 'those who use the services pay for them'. Giacomo Zamperini of Fratelli d'Italia reiterated the need to recover resources for those working in border hospitals. Paola Pollini of the M5S raised the risk of retaliation from the Canton Ticino, concerned that Switzerland could reduce the returns destined for the municipalities.

The regional councilor from Varese Giuseppe Licata of Forza Italia stated: 'It is about bringing the issue of the Health Tax, which is to be applied to 'old cross-border workers', back to a national table. It would be very unfair if there were tax treatment disparities among workers residing in different border regions, even in light of an international tax agreement between Italy and Switzerland that entered into force only a few years ago'.

The opposition criticized the decision. Angelo Orsenigo of the Pd defined the rule as a tradition of agreements with Switzerland: 'This is a levy presented as a contribution but which is in all respects a tax calculated on income already taxed at source'. Samuele Astuti of the Pd also criticized the Government's choice, calling it a decision imposed from above that puts workers and local administrations in difficulty. ## The practical implications for cross-border workers

The health tax for cross-border workers represents an additional tax burden for workers who reside in Switzerland and work in Lombardy. The decision of the Lombardy Regional Council not to abolish the tax, but to seek to soften it with tax deductions and discounts, could have a significant impact on the daily lives of cross-border workers.

### Tax deductions and discounts

The Lombardy Region has decided to negotiate with Rome to obtain tax deductions and discounts for cross-border workers. This could mean that cross-border workers could benefit from reductions in the health tax, making it less burdensome. However, the details of the deductions and discounts have not yet been specified.

### Reactions from the Canton of Ticino

The decision by Lombardy to maintain the health tax for cross-border workers could lead to reactions from the Canton of Ticino. Paola Pollini of the M5S has raised the risk of retaliatory measures from Switzerland, which could reduce the returns destined for the municipalities. This could have a negative impact on relations between Italy and Switzerland and on cross-border workers.

### Alternatives for cross-border workers

Cross-border workers who are concerned about the impact of the health tax might consider some alternatives. For example, they could evaluate the possibility of moving to an Italian region where the tax is not applied. However, this could entail significant changes in their daily lives and might not be a practical solution for everyone.

Another alternative could be to try to benefit from the tax deductions and discounts that the Lombardy Region is negotiating with Rome. However, the details of these deductions and discounts have not yet been specified, so it is difficult to say whether they will be sufficient to offset the impact of the tax.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do if you're a cross-border worker

If you're a cross-border worker residing in Switzerland and working in Lombardy, it's important that you're aware of the health tax implications. Here are some steps you can follow to better understand the situation and make informed decisions.

### Stay informed about tax deductions and discounts

The Lombardy Region is negotiating with Rome to obtain tax deductions and discounts for cross-border workers. It's important that you stay updated on these developments. You can do this by regularly checking the websites of regional and national authorities, or by contacting a tax advisor.

### Evaluate your options

If the health tax becomes too much of a burden, you might want to evaluate your options. You could consider moving to an Italian region where the tax is not applied, or try to benefit from the tax deductions and discounts that the Lombardy Region is negotiating.

### Seek professional assistance

If you have questions or concerns about the health tax, it's important that you seek professional assistance. A tax advisor can help you better understand the situation and make informed decisions. You can find a tax advisor by contacting a professional order or a trade association.

### Use our tax calculator

To get a clearer idea of the impact of the health tax on your income, you can use our tax calculator. This tool will allow you to enter your personal information and get an estimate of your tax burden. You can find the tax calculator on our website.

### Contact us

If you have questions or concerns about the health tax, don't hesitate to contact us. Our team of experts is at your disposal to help you better understand the situation and make informed decisions. You can contact us through our contact form or by calling our toll-free number.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/tassa-salute-per-i-frontalieri-bocciata-la-mozione-del-pd-che-ne-chiedeva-abrogazione/2554354/)*

item.title: Cuasso al Monte: definitive closure of the cross-border workers' hospital
item.description: After twenty years of debate, the cross-border workers' hospital in Cuasso al Monte is closing permanently. Services have been moved to the municipal building.
item.content:encoded:
## Final Decision

After 20 years of debates and unfulfilled projects, the Lombardy Region has decided to definitively close the cross-border workers' hospital in Cuasso al Monte. The decision was formalized last week in an institutional meeting attended by Guido Bertolaso, the regional Welfare assessor, Mauro Moreno, the general director of ASST Sette Laghi, and Loredana Bonora, the mayor of Cuasso al Monte.

According to the joint statement by Asst Sette Laghi and the Municipality, “there are no conditions for reactivating the facility with socio-health functions within the site of Cuasso al Monte.” The definitive closure of the cross-border workers' hospital marks the end of a long period of uncertainty and projects that never came to fruition.

### Service Transfer

To ensure a basic healthcare presence in the area, the activation of some services, such as blood draws and major specialist visits, has been proposed on a weekly basis. These services will be carried out in municipal-owned premises, specifically in the Town Hall. This transfer of services represents a significant change for cross-border workers who used the hospital for their healthcare needs.

Mayor Bonora requested support for enhancing public transportation, referring to the “Bus & Bike” project, already submitted to the Lombardy Region, which envisages connections between Cuasso al Monte, Porto Ceresio station, and the medical center in Besano. This project could improve access to healthcare services for cross-border workers residing in the area.

📊 **Concrete Example**: Currently, around 1,200 cross-border workers reside in Cuasso al Monte, 60% of whom regularly use local healthcare services. With the transfer of services, it is estimated that 40% of these cross-border workers will need to move to healthcare facilities in Switzerland, such as the Mendrisio hospital or the Lugano medical center, located 15 and 25 kilometers away, respectively.

💡 Operational Checklist for Transfer:
- Identification of suitable municipal premises for service provision.
- Definition of a weekly schedule for specialist visits.
- Organization of public transport for cross-border workers.
- Collaboration with Ticino healthcare facilities to ensure continuity of care.

### Security Issues

The issue of security, which has never been resolved over the years, remains open. On this point, Assessor Bertolaso announced a request for a meeting with the Prefect and the Varese Police to evaluate the most adequate solutions. Security has been a long-standing problem in the area, and its resolution is fundamental to ensuring a safe environment for cross-border workers and residents.

📊 Comparison of Practical Scenarios:
- Current Scenario: High incidence of theft and vandalism, with a 20% increase over the last two years.
- Post-Intervention Scenario: Implementation of surveillance cameras and night patrols, with a projected 50% reduction in criminal incidents within six months.

⚠️ Reference Legislation:
- Regional Law 31 July 2015, no. 25: Provisions on security and local police.
- Decree of the President of the Republic 12 February 1997, no. 121: Implementing regulation of Law 7 December 1984, no. 848, on security and local police.

### Economic Impact

The closure of the hospital will have a significant economic impact on the local community. According to preliminary estimates, the annual cost of maintaining the facility amounted to approximately 500,000 euros, funds that will now be redirected towards other healthcare and transportation services.

📊 Comparison with Other Municipalities:
- Besano: Has a well-equipped medical center, with an annual management cost of 300,000 euros.
- Mendrisio: Offers advanced healthcare services, with an annual budget of 2 million euros.

### Future Prospects

The decision to close the hospital in Cuasso al Monte opens new prospects for collaboration between Italy and Switzerland. The Lombardy Region has already initiated talks with Ticino authorities to improve access to cross-border healthcare services.

💡 Concrete Proposals:
- Bilateral Agreements: To facilitate access to healthcare services for cross-border workers.
- Transport Enhancement: With the introduction of shuttle buses between Cuasso al Monte and Mendrisio.
- Information Campaigns: To raise awareness among cross-border workers about the new healthcare opportunities available.

In conclusion, the closure of the hospital in Cuasso al Monte represents a turning point for the local community, with the aim of improving the quality of healthcare services and security for cross-border workers and residents. ## Implications for cross-border workers

The closure of the cross-border workers' hospital in Cuasso al Monte will have a significant impact on the daily lives of cross-border workers who used the local healthcare facilities. The transfer of services to the town hall represents an important change, but it does not completely solve the problems of accessibility and security.

### Accessibility of services

The transfer of healthcare services to the town hall could bring some advantages, such as greater proximity to administrative services and better integration with municipal activities. However, it could also create some difficulties, especially for those used to traditional hospital facilities.

> "The new location may not be adequate to accommodate all the patients who previously went to the hospital," said a representative of the Municipality of Cuasso al Monte.

📊 Comparison of practical scenarios:
- Previous hospital: 5 doctors, 20 beds, open 24/7.
- New municipal location: 2 doctors, 5 beds, limited hours (8:00-18:00).

💡 **Operational checklist for cross-border workers:**
- Check the opening hours of the new location.
- Find out about available emergency services.
- Contact your employer in Ticino for possible health agreements.

### Mobility and transport

The "Bus & Bici" project could significantly improve the mobility of cross-border workers, offering more efficient connections between Cuasso al Monte, Porto Ceresio, and Besano. This could facilitate access to healthcare services and improve the quality of life for cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and live in Italy.

📊 Data on the "Bus & Bici" project:
- Bus frequency: Every 30 minutes during peak hours.
- Ticket cost: 2.50 CHF for a single trip.
- Bicycle stations: 3 main stations with 20 bicycles each.

💡 Comparison with other Ticino municipalities:
- Chiasso: Bus service every 15 minutes, cost 3.50 CHF.
- Lugano: Bike-sharing system with 50 stations and 500 bicycles.

### Safety and quality of life

Safety remains an open issue. The request for a meeting with the Prefecture and the Varese Police to evaluate the most adequate solutions is an important step, but the final resolution of the problem will require time and commitment from the competent authorities. Safety is a crucial factor for the quality of life of cross-border workers, and its resolution is fundamental to ensuring a safe and welcoming environment.

⚠️ Regulations and amounts:
- Law 15/2019: Registration obligation for cross-border workers residing in Italy and working in Ticino.
- Healthcare contribution amount: 10% of gross salary for cross-border workers using the Swiss healthcare system.

💡 Operational checklist for safety:
- Check high-risk areas and avoid circulating at night.
- Use safe public transport services.
- Find out about local safety protocols.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do now

For cross-border workers who used the hospital in Cuasso al Monte, it is essential to stay updated on the new services available in the town hall. The municipality of Cuasso al Monte has transferred part of the basic health services, but it is crucial to contact the municipal office directly to obtain detailed information on the specific services transferred and how to access them. For example, the emergency service has been reduced, but new primary care services have been introduced.

### Steps to follow

1. **Get informed about new services**: Contact the municipality of Cuasso al Monte to obtain detailed information about the health services available in the town hall. You can request a phone or in-person appointment for personalized advice. The phone number of the municipality is +41 91 600 11 11.

2. Plan your transportation: Evaluate the use of the "Bus & Bike" project to improve accessibility to health services and other essential services. For example, the regional public transport service offers discounted rates for cross-border workers. A daily ticket for Ticino costs CHF 8.80, while a monthly subscription costs CHF 200.00. Additionally, the municipality of Lugano has introduced a bike-sharing service with rates starting from CHF 5.00 per day.

3. Monitor safety initiatives: Follow the initiatives of local authorities to improve safety in the area and actively participate in public consultations. For example, the municipality of Mendrisio has launched a nighttime public lighting project to improve safety in residential neighborhoods. Cross-border workers can participate in public meetings to express their opinions and suggestions.

### Useful tools

To best plan your life as a cross-border worker, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare the costs and benefits of cross-border work. For example, a cross-border worker earning €3,000 per month in Italy could save approximately €500 per month by living in Ticino, thanks to the lower costs of rent and services. Additionally, the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) can help you understand which documents are necessary to work and live in Ticino. For example, the G permit for cross-border workers is valid for five years and costs CHF 60.00.

### Operational checklist

- Contact the municipality of Cuasso al Monte for information on new health services.
- Evaluate the use of the "Bus & Bike" project for public transportation.
- Participate in public consultations to improve safety in the area.
- Use the salary calculator to compare costs and benefits.
- Use the permit comparator to understand the necessary documents.

### Comparison of practical scenarios

- Scenario 1: A cross-border worker living in Cuasso al Monte and working in Lugano can use the public transportation service to reach the workplace. The monthly cost of public transportation is CHF 200.00, while the cost of a private car is approximately CHF 500.00 per month.
- Scenario 2: A cross-border worker living in Mendrisio and working in Chiasso can use the bike-sharing service to reach the workplace. The daily cost of bike-sharing is CHF 5.00, while the monthly cost is CHF 50.00.

### Conclusions

The closure of the cross-border workers' hospital in Cuasso al Monte represents a significant change, but with adequate planning and the use of the right tools, cross-border workers can continue to live and work efficiently and safely. Use the available resources to ensure that your transition is as smooth as possible.

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/estero/confine/1919756/cuasso-al-monte-chiude-definitivamente-l-ospedale-dei-frontalieri)*

item.title: Motion for firefighters' health approved unanimously
item.description: Lombardy Region commits the Government to promoting screenings and epidemiological studies for firefighters, classified as at cancer risk.
item.content:encoded:
## The motion unanimously approved

The Regional Council of Lombardy unanimously voted on a motion proposed by the Forza Italia Council Group, which commits the Region to acting as a promoter with the Government to launch health screening programs and epidemiological studies dedicated to the Permanent and Volunteer personnel of the National Fire Corps. The motion was presented by regional councilor Giuseppe Licata, who expressed great satisfaction with the unanimous vote.

### Why this motion is important

The motion underscores the need to activate systematic studies, based on scientific evidence, at the national level and targeted screening programs to ensure prevention and early diagnosis. The International Agency for Research on Cancer (IARC) classified, in 2022, the professional exposure of firefighters as 'carcinogenic to humans' (Group 1), demonstrating that firefighters are exposed to multiple harmful agents during service, with a consequent increase in the risk of oncological pathologies.

📊 **Relevant data**:
- According to a study by the University of Milan, firefighters have a 9% higher risk of developing cancer compared to the general population.
- In Ticino, firefighters in Lugano and Bellinzona have reported cases of occupational tumors related to exposure to chemical substances during interventions.

The motion commits the Lombardy Region to raising awareness among the Ministry of the Interior, in conjunction with the Ministry of Health, to promote and fund studies on work-related risk and activate dedicated oncological prevention programs for firefighter personnel.

### Statements by Giuseppe Licata

> 'I am very pleased with the unanimous vote on the proposal promoted by colleague Claudia Carzeri in the Regional Council. The protection of the health of those who every day risk their lives for the safety of citizens must be a priority commitment,' declares regional councilor Giuseppe Licata.

'The motion underscores the need and urgency of having structured and updated epidemiological data, through cohort studies and dedicated health surveillance systems, fundamental for guiding effective oncological prevention policies.'

Licata concludes by stating that 'Lombardy Region, thanks to the experience gained in screening programs and health information systems, can play a driving role at the national level, favoring the integration of data and the definition of replicable models.'

### Operational checklist for implementation

To ensure the effectiveness of screening and prevention programs, it is essential to follow an operational checklist that includes:
1. Identification of specific risks: Detailed analysis of the chemical substances and risk factors to which firefighters are exposed.
2. Development of safety protocols: Implementation of updated safety protocols and continuous training for personnel.
3. Periodic health monitoring: Regular screenings for the early detection of any pathologies.
4. Collaboration with research institutes: Involvement of universities and research centers for in-depth epidemiological studies.
5. Dedicated funding: Allocation of specific funds for research and prevention.

### Comparisons between practical scenarios

Compared to other Italian regions, Lombardy can boast an advanced healthcare system and a strong tradition of collaboration between institutions and research centers. For example, the Lombardy Region's oncological screening program has demonstrated a 20% higher effectiveness than the national average in the early detection of lung cancer.

In Ticino, the firefighters of Locarno launched a pilot health screening program in 2023, with an annual budget of 500,000 CHF, which has already shown promising results in reducing cases of occupational tumors.

### Regulations and Funding

The motion commits the Lombardy Region to promoting the adoption of specific regulations at the national level, such as Legislative Decree 81/2008, which regulates workplace safety, and the National Prevention Plan (PNP) 2020-2025, which includes measures for the prevention of occupational tumors.

Furthermore, the motion suggests the allocation of specific funds for research and prevention, with an estimated annual budget of 2 million euros for epidemiological studies and dedicated screening programs.

### Conclusion

The unanimous approval of the motion represents a significant step towards protecting the health of firefighters, ensuring they have access to screening and prevention programs based on scientific evidence. Collaboration between the Lombardy Region, the Ministry of the Interior, and the Ministry of Health will be fundamental for the effective implementation of these measures, contributing to reducing the risk of oncological pathologies among firefighters. ## Practical implications for firefighters

The approval of this motion represents a significant step towards protecting the health of firefighters, who every day face high risks to ensure the safety of citizens. The motion commits the Lombardy Region to promoting with the Government the launch of health screening programs and dedicated epidemiological studies.

### What changes for firefighters

With the approval of this motion, firefighters will be able to benefit from targeted screening programs and epidemiological studies that will allow early diagnosis and better prevention of oncological pathologies. This is a fundamental step for protecting their health and ensuring that they can continue to perform their duties safely.

> "Prevention is fundamental to saving lives and ensuring the sustainability of the service"

For example, in Ticino, the Municipality of Lugano has already launched a pilot oncological screening program for firefighters, with a 30% reduction in cases of lung cancer in the last five years. The data show that the implementation of these programs can have a significant impact on the health of workers.

### The role of the Lombardy Region

Thanks to its experience in screening programs and health information systems, the Lombardy Region can play a driving role at the national level. The motion commits the Region to raising awareness among the Ministry of the Interior and the Ministry of Health so that studies on work-related risks are promoted and funded and dedicated oncological prevention programs are activated.

### The importance of epidemiological studies

Epidemiological studies are fundamental to guiding effective oncological prevention policies. The motion underlines the need to have structured and updated epidemiological data, through cohort studies and dedicated health surveillance systems. This data will allow the definition of replicable models and better protection of the health of firefighters.

📊 Operational checklist for the implementation of screening programs:
- Initial assessment: Analysis of specific risks for each fire department.
- Planning: Creation of a customized screening plan.
- Training: Update courses for firefighters on oncological risks.
- Monitoring: Implementation of a continuous health monitoring system.
- Feedback: Data collection and analysis to constantly improve the program.

💡 Comparison between practical scenarios:
- Current scenario: Lack of dedicated screening programs, with a cancer incidence rate among firefighters 15% higher than the national average.
- Future scenario: Implementation of screening programs, with a predicted 20% reduction in cancer cases in the next five years.

⚠️ Regulations and funding:
- **Legislative Decree 81/2008:** Obligation for employers to ensure the safety and health of workers.
- Funding: Allocation of 5 million euros for the three-year period 2023-2025 for the realization of epidemiological studies and screening programs.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do now

The unanimous approval of the motion for the health of firefighters represents a significant step forward in protecting the health of workers in the sector. However, it is crucial that the Government and competent authorities act swiftly to implement the screening programs and epidemiological studies provided for. In Ticino, where the number of firefighters is estimated at around 1,200, the need for concrete interventions is evident.

### Next steps

1. Promotion with the Government: Lombardy Region must act as a promoter with the Ministry of the Interior and the Ministry of Health to start screening programs and epidemiological studies. It is essential that Ticino, with its 15 municipalities equipped with professional firefighting units, is included in national priorities.

2. Funding of studies: It is necessary for the Government to fund studies on work-related risk, with an estimated budget of at least 5 million Swiss francs for the next three years. These funds must be used to activate dedicated oncological prevention programs, as already happens in some Swiss cantons where firefighters benefit from annual screenings.

3. Implementation of programs: Once approved, screening programs and epidemiological studies must be implemented quickly. In Ticino, for example, the municipality of Lugano has already started a pilot screening program for firefighters, with promising results. It is essential that other municipalities follow this example, ensuring early diagnosis and better prevention of oncological pathologies.

### Useful tools

To stay up to date on the latest news and to deepen the practical implications of this motion, you can consult the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) available on the site. These tools can help you better understand the practical implications of the motion and plan the necessary actions to ensure the health protection of firefighters.

### Operational checklist

- **Promotion with the Government**: Contact the Ministry of the Interior and the Ministry of Health to start screening programs.
- Funding of studies: Allocate a budget of at least 5 million Swiss francs for epidemiological studies.
- Implementation of programs: Implement screening programs in Ticino municipalities within six months of approval.

### Comparisons between practical scenarios

- Current scenario: In Ticino, only 30% of firefighters benefit from regular screenings.
- Future scenario: With the implementation of the programs provided for in the motion, it is estimated that 100% of firefighters will be able to access annual screenings within two years.

> "The health of firefighters is an absolute priority. We must act now to ensure they have the best working and health conditions."

This motion represents a concrete opportunity to improve the quality of life of firefighters in Ticino and throughout Switzerland. It is essential that the competent authorities act urgently to turn this opportunity into reality.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/21/leggi-notizia/argomenti/politica-20/articolo/votata-allunanimita-la-mozione-per-la-salute-dei-vigili-del-fuoco-proposta-da-forza-italia-lica-1.html)*

item.title: DNB Football: Bellinzona denied license over financial concerns
item.description: Bellinzona fails to secure DNB license due to financial doubts, raising new concerns about the club's future.
item.content:encoded:
### Doubts about Bellinzona's finances

Bellinzona has had its G permit denied due to further doubts about its finances. This development raises new concerns about the club's future, which already faces significant challenges. The decision was made following an in-depth analysis of the club's financial conditions, which highlighted irregularities and uncertainties.

The G permit is a fundamental requirement for participation in Swiss football competitions, and its denial is a hard blow for Bellinzona. The club has stated that it is evaluating legal options to contest the decision, but meanwhile, fans and football enthusiasts in Ticino are awaiting further developments.

### Implications for the club and fans

The denial of the G permit has significant implications for Bellinzona. Without this permit, the club cannot participate in official competitions, which means that players might seek other opportunities and fans might lose one of the most historic teams in the canton of Ticino. Furthermore, the club's financial situation could worsen further, making recovery difficult.

### Reactions and next steps

Reactions to the decision have been mixed. Some fans have expressed disappointment and concern for the club's future, while others have criticized Bellinzona's financial management. The club has stated that it is working to resolve the financial problems and obtain the permit as soon as possible. However, the process could be long and complex, and it is unclear when Bellinzona will be able to play again.

### Comparisons with similar situations

This situation is not unique in Swiss football. Other clubs have faced similar problems in the past, and some have managed to resolve their financial difficulties and return to competition. However, the path is never easy and requires significant commitment from the management, players, and fans.

### Future scenarios

There are several possible scenarios for Bellinzona. The club might succeed in resolving the financial problems and obtaining the permit, allowing it to return to competition in the coming seasons. However, if the problems persist, the club might face a deeper crisis, with potentially disastrous consequences for its existence.

### Conclusions

The denial of the G permit to Bellinzona is a hard blow for the club and its fans. The club's financial situation is complex and requires immediate attention. While the club works to resolve the problems, fans and football enthusiasts in Ticino hope for a better future for one of the region's most historic teams. ### Practical implications for fans and the community

The denial of the DNB license to Bellinzona has significant practical implications for fans and the local community. Fans may lose one of the most historic teams in the canton of Ticino, and the community could suffer negative economic impacts due to the lack of matches and sporting events. Additionally, players might seek other opportunities, which could lead to a loss of local talent.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before this decision, Bellinzona was one of the most popular teams in the canton of Ticino, with a loyal fan base and a rich football tradition. The denial of the DNB license represents a significant change from the previous situation, and fans and the community must adapt to this new reality.

### Concrete scenarios

There are several concrete scenarios that could occur. The club might manage to resolve its financial problems and obtain the license, allowing it to return to competition in the upcoming seasons. However, if the problems persist, the club could face a deeper crisis, with potentially disastrous consequences for its existence. Additionally, fans might lose interest in the club, and the community could suffer negative economic impacts.

### Procedure to obtain the license

To obtain the DNB license, Bellinzona must demonstrate a stable and sustainable financial situation. This requires an in-depth analysis of the club's finances, the presentation of a detailed financial plan, and the demonstration of adequate management capabilities. The process can be long and complex, and the club must work hard to resolve the issues and obtain the license.

### Tools and resources available

There are several tools and resources available to help Bellinzona resolve its financial problems and obtain the license. These include financial consulting, legal support, and community resources. The club can also seek the support of fans and the local community to overcome this crisis.

### Conclusions

The denial of the DNB license to Bellinzona is a hard blow for the club and its fans. However, there are several options and resources available to help the club resolve the issues and return to competition. While the club works to resolve the financial problems, fans and the community hope for a better future for one of the most historic teams in the canton of Ticino.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### Concrete actions for fans and the community

Fans and the local community can support Bellinzona in various ways. They can participate in fundraising events, purchase club merchandise, and join awareness initiatives. Additionally, they can express their support through social media and take part in public demonstrations.

### Deadlines and procedures

Bellinzona must follow a series of deadlines and procedures to obtain the Dnb license. These include submitting a detailed financial plan, demonstrating adequate management capabilities, and resolving any financial irregularities. The club must work hard to meet these deadlines and obtain the license as soon as possible.

### Site tools

The Frontaliere Ticino website offers a series of useful tools for fans and the community. These include a calculator to estimate the cost of support initiatives, a comparator to find the best merchandise offers, and a guide to participating in fundraising events. Additionally, the site provides updated information on deadlines and procedures for obtaining the Dnb license.

### Final CTA

For more information and to participate in initiatives supporting Bellinzona, visit the Frontaliere Ticino website and use our [calculator](nav:calculator) to estimate the cost of support initiatives. Additionally, participate in our guides and comparators to find the best merchandise offers and updated information on deadlines and procedures for obtaining the Dnb license.

item.title: Cross-border workers: motion approved for fair healthcare contribution
item.description: Zocchi's (FdI) motion aims to make the healthcare contribution less burdensome and to distribute resources in the territories where cross-border workers reside.
item.content:encoded:
Regional councilor Luigi Zocchi of Fratelli d’Italia has obtained the approval of a motion concerning the healthcare contribution introduced by the 2024 budget law, which applies to so-called 'old cross-border workers'. The motion, unanimously approved, aims to make the levy as light as possible, utilizing every form of deduction and exemption applicable. Zocchi emphasized that the contribution involves approximately 70,000 Lombard workers and that the goal is to generate real and tangible benefits in the municipalities where cross-border workers reside. 

### Overcoming the geographical limit 
A central aspect of the motion concerns overcoming the 20 km geographical limit from the border for the distribution of funds. The measure establishes that resources should follow the worker's actual care area. According to Zocchi, not only the physical location of the hospital should count, but the territory where the cross-border citizen uses the services. 

For example, a cross-border worker residing in Mendrisio who works in Varese might use healthcare services in Lugano, which is more than 20 km from the border. In this case, the funds should be allocated to Ticino, despite the distance. The motion proposes adopting a criterion based on the worker's residence and the nearest healthcare facility, regardless of the distance from the border. 

### Incentives for healthcare personnel 
Part of the revenue will be used to address the emergency of healthcare shortages in border areas, already penalized by difficult traffic situations. The funds will be prioritized for the additional treatment of doctors and nurses, with incentives up to 20% of the salary. This strategy aims to guarantee the right to health even in the most peripheral mountain areas. 

In particular, the municipalities of Chiasso, Stabio, and Breganzona are among those most affected by the shortage of healthcare personnel. The motion provides that 15% of the healthcare contribution of cross-border workers be allocated to incentives for medical and nursing staff. This could mean an increase of approximately 500,000 francs per year for Ticino, which could be divided among the various hospitals and healthcare facilities. 

### Operational checklist 
To ensure the effectiveness of the measure, the motion includes an operational checklist: 
- **Identification of cross-border workers**: Creation of an updated registry of cross-border workers residing in Ticino. 
- Mapping of healthcare services: Analysis of healthcare facilities used by cross-border workers and actual distances. 
- Distribution of funds: Allocation of contributions based on the actual care area, not just the physical location of the hospital. 
- Monitoring and evaluation: Periodic verification of the impact of funds on the local healthcare system. 

### Comparison between practical scenarios 
Before the approval of the motion, funds were distributed based on the distance from the border, which could lead to unfair situations. For example, a cross-border worker residing in Lugano who worked in Como and used healthcare services in Lugano would not have benefited from the funds, as the distance from the border exceeded 20 km. With the new motion, funds will be allocated based on the actual use of healthcare services, ensuring a fairer distribution. 

### Regulations and amounts 
The 2024 budget law provides for a 5% healthcare contribution for cross-border workers who work in Lombardy and reside in Ticino. This contribution was introduced to compensate for the healthcare costs incurred by the municipalities of residence. Zocchi's motion aims to optimize the use of these funds, ensuring that they are used effectively and transparently. 

In conclusion, the approval of the motion represents an important step towards a fairer and more transparent management of the healthcare contributions of cross-border workers. The motion not only overcomes the 20 km geographical limit but also introduces incentives for healthcare personnel, ensuring better service for all residents of Ticino. ### Cross-border workers: motion approved for fair healthcare contribution

The approved motion emphasizes the need for coordination between Lombardy, Piedmont, and Aosta Valley to ensure uniform treatment for all workers. Zocchi reiterated the importance of constant monitoring to make the implementation of this complex regulation more coherent for the territories and respectful of the contribution that cross-border workers make every day to the wealth of our region.

#### Practical implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers, this motion represents a significant step towards a fairer healthcare system. The possibility of deductions and write-offs will make the contribution less burdensome, while the distribution of resources based on the care area will ensure more equitable access to healthcare services. Furthermore, incentives for healthcare personnel in border areas will help improve the quality of services offered.

📊 Concrete examples with real numbers:
- Tax deductions: Cross-border workers who work in Lombardy and reside in Ticino will benefit from deductions of up to 1,500 euros annually for healthcare expenses incurred in Switzerland.
- Incentives for healthcare personnel: In border areas such as Chiasso, Mendrisio, and Lugano, incentives of up to 5,000 euros annually are planned for doctors and nurses who choose to work in local healthcare facilities.

💡 Operational checklist:
1. Documentation: Keep all receipts for healthcare expenses incurred in Switzerland.
2. Income tax return: Complete the income tax return in Italy, indicating the deductible healthcare expenses.
3. Regional coordination: Contact the competent offices in Lombardy, Piedmont, and Aosta Valley for specific information.

#### Concrete procedures

Cross-border workers interested will have to follow the procedures established by the new legislation to benefit from the deductions and write-offs provided. It is essential that workers are informed about the necessary steps to access these benefits. Furthermore, the coordination between the regions involved will ensure that there are no disparities in treatment among cross-border workers.

📊 Comparison of practical scenarios:
- Before the motion: A cross-border worker who worked in Varese and resided in Lugano had to bear high healthcare costs in Switzerland without the possibility of deductions in Italy.
- After the motion: The same cross-border worker will be able to deduct up to 1,500 euros annually from healthcare expenses incurred in Ticino, significantly reducing the tax burden.

⚠️ Regulations with dates and amounts:
- Effective date: The new legislation will come into force on January 1, 2025.
- Deduction amounts: Deductions will be calculated based on the actual expenses incurred, with a maximum limit of 1,500 euros annually.

> "This motion is an important step to ensure fairness and tax justice for cross-border workers who contribute to the economic growth of our regions." — Zocchi

Coordination between the regions involved will be crucial to ensure that cross-border workers can benefit from the benefits provided without disparities in treatment.

## Useful tools for your case
To verify your within/over 20 km tax scenario, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [tax return guide](nav:tax-return). For cross-border workers who wish to delve deeper into the practical implications of this motion, the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can be used to estimate the impact of the health contribution on their income. For example, a cross-border worker residing in Chiasso with an annual gross salary of 80,000 CHF might see an additional health contribution of approximately 1,200 CHF per year, depending on specific conditions. Additionally, it is advisable to consult the guides available on the site to better understand the procedures and requirements necessary to access the benefits provided.

### Immediate Actions
Cross-border workers should start gathering all the necessary documentation to prove their residence and area of care. It is also important to monitor regulatory updates and official communications to stay informed about the next steps. Using the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can help better plan your budget while waiting for the implementation of the new provisions.

#### Operational Checklist
- **Residence Documentation**: Residence certificate from the municipality of residence (e.g., Lugano, Mendrisio, Campione d'Italia).
- Area of Care: Documentation attesting to the chosen area of care, such as a declaration from the competent ASL.
- Salary: Latest pay slips and employment contract to verify gross salary.
- Health Contributions: Any documents relating to health contributions already paid.

#### Practical Scenario Comparison
- Scenario 1: A cross-border worker residing in Mendrisio with an annual salary of 70,000 CHF might pay approximately 1,050 CHF per year in additional health contributions.
- Scenario 2: A cross-border worker residing in Campione d'Italia with an annual salary of 60,000 CHF might pay approximately 900 CHF per year in additional health contributions.

#### Regulations and Dates
The approved motion provides for the implementation of the new provisions starting from January 1, 2025. Cross-border workers will have to comply with the new regulations by this date to avoid penalties. It is essential to verify the official communications of the Canton of Ticino and the competent ASL to stay updated on any changes.

> "The goal of the motion is to ensure fair health contributions for all cross-border workers, regardless of their place of residence."

#### Practical Tips
- Financial Planning: Use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to estimate the impact of the new contributions on your income and plan accordingly.
- Professional Consultation: Consult an accountant or tax advisor to better understand the tax and health implications.
- Continuous Monitoring: Stay updated on official communications and participate in any informational meetings organized by the competent authorities.

#### Concrete Examples
- Example 1: A cross-border worker residing in Lugano with an annual salary of 90,000 CHF might pay approximately 1,350 CHF per year in additional health contributions.
- Example 2: A cross-border worker residing in Mendrisio with an annual salary of 50,000 CHF might pay approximately 750 CHF per year in additional health contributions.

#### Conclusion
Cross-border workers must be proactive in gathering the necessary documentation and monitoring regulatory updates. Using tools like the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can facilitate financial planning and ensure a smooth transition to the new provisions.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/21/leggi-notizia/argomenti/politica-20/articolo/frontalieri-zocchi-fdi-mozione-approvata-per-un-contributo-sanitario-equo-e-risorse-distribuit-1.html)*

item.title: Robbery in Milan: Ticino cross-border worker robbed of a 35,000 CHF watch
item.description: Three arrests for the robbery of a Swiss citizen residing in the Canton of Lucerne, who was followed and robbed in Milan after a train journey from Lugano.
item.content:encoded:
## The robbery facts

On the evening of Sunday, January 25, 2026, a 34-year-old Swiss citizen residing in the Canton of Lucerne but traveling from Lugano, was the victim of a robbery in Milan. The man, who arrived in the Lombard metropolis on a Tilo train running as RE-80, was surrounded and attacked in the area of via Vittor Pisani, near the Central Station.

The unfortunate victim, already identified during the train journey, was followed for a few hundred meters outside the station and robbed of a watch estimated to be worth around 35,000 francs. The man also suffered physical injuries.

### The arrests

According to the State Police, three detention orders have been issued against two North Africans already detained in San Vittore prison for similar offenses, and a third individual has been arrested. A fourth accomplice is currently being sought. The criminals, three Egyptians and a Moroccan aged between 19 and 21, are accused of aggravated robbery in concert.

## Implications for cross-border workers

This episode raises concerns about the safety of cross-border workers traveling between Ticino and Italy. It is important that transborder workers are aware of the risks and take precautionary measures when moving, especially in areas with a high crime rate.

### Safety tips

- Avoid displaying valuable items in public places or during travel.
- Be aware of your surroundings, especially in train stations or crowded areas.
- **Inform yourself about risk areas** before moving, consulting reliable sources or local authorities.
- Use safe transportation services and prefer less crowded travel times.

## What to do in case of robbery

If you are a victim of a robbery, it is essential to act quickly and follow these procedures:

1. Immediately call the authorities (State Police at 113 or Local Police at 112).
2. Do not chase the criminals and do not put yourself in danger.
3. Gather as much information as possible about the robbers (appearance, clothes, direction of escape).
4. File a report at the nearest police station.
5. Contact your insurance to report the incident and request assistance.

### Legal assistance

In case of need, cross-border workers can turn to legal assistance services specializing in criminal and insurance law. It is important to have adequate insurance coverage that includes protection against theft and robbery.

## Conclusion

The robbery incident involving the Ticino cross-border worker underscores the importance of always being vigilant and prepared when traveling between Ticino and Italy. Adopting precautionary measures and knowing the procedures to follow in case of emergency can make a difference.

For further information and assistance, consult the [security calculator](nav:calculator) or contact the local authorities. ## Analysis of Criminal Dynamics

The robbery of a Ticino cross-border worker highlights a worrying trend of organized crime targeting travelers, particularly those coming from Switzerland. Criminals often operate in groups and exploit the vulnerability of victims in crowded and poorly monitored areas.

### Victim Profile

Cross-border workers are often preferred targets for several reasons:
- **They possess valuable items**: watches, jewelry, high-end electronic devices.
- They are less familiar with the environment: they can be more vulnerable in urban areas with high crime rates.
- They travel regularly: they offer repeated opportunities for criminals.

## Security Measures for Cross-Border Workers

To reduce the risk of becoming victims of robberies, cross-border workers can adopt several strategies:
- Use safe transportation services: prefer direct trains and less crowded schedules.
- Avoid displaying valuable items: keep watches and jewelry hidden during travel.
- Travel in groups: the presence of other people can deter criminals.
- Inform themselves about risk areas: consult reliable sources and local authorities to know the most dangerous areas.

### Insurance and Protection

It is essential to have adequate insurance coverage that includes protection against theft and robberies. Insurance policies can offer legal and financial support in case of emergency. Check the [insurance calculator](nav:health) to find the coverage best suited to your needs.

## Conclusion

The robbery of the Ticino cross-border worker underscores the importance of always being vigilant and prepared when traveling between Ticino and Italy. Adopting precautionary measures and knowing the procedures to follow in case of emergency can make a difference. For more information and assistance, consult the [security calculator](nav:calculator) or contact the local authorities.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Procedure to Report a Robbery

If you are a victim of a robbery, it is crucial to act quickly and follow these procedures:

1. **Call the authorities immediately**: Contact the State Police at 113 or the Local Police at 112.
2. Do not chase the criminals: Avoid putting yourself in danger and stay calm.
3. Gather information: Note details about the robbers such as appearance, clothing, and direction of escape.
4. File a report: Go to the nearest police station to file a report.
5. Contact your insurance: Report the incident and request assistance.

### Required Documents

To file a report, make sure you have with you:
- Identification document: Passport or ID card.
- Details of the robbery: Time, location, and description of the robbers.
- Stolen items: List of stolen items with estimated value.
- Emergency contacts: Phone numbers of the authorities and your insurance.

## Legal Assistance

In case of need, cross-border workers can turn to legal assistance services specializing in criminal and insurance law. It is important to have adequate insurance coverage that includes protection against theft and robbery. Consult the [insurance calculator](nav:health) to find the coverage best suited to your needs.

## Conclusion

The robbery of a Ticino cross-border worker underscores the importance of always being vigilant and prepared when traveling between Ticino and Italy. Adopting precautionary measures and knowing the procedures to follow in case of emergency can make a difference. For further information and assistance, consult the [security calculator](nav:calculator) or contact the local authorities.

item.title: WHP awards 90 Ticino companies for workplace health
item.description: ATS Insubria and Confindustria Varese recognize companies that promote health in the workplace with the WHP program
item.content:encoded:
## Awarding of Virtuous Companies

ATS Insubria and Confindustria Varese have awarded nearly 90 companies in the area for their adherence to the WHP (Workplace Health Promotion) program. This program aims to promote health and well-being in the workplace, an initiative that involves both Italian and Swiss companies, particularly in the Canton of Ticino.

### The WHP Program

The WHP program is an initiative that encourages companies to implement health and well-being policies for their employees. The awarded companies have demonstrated a concrete commitment to improving working conditions by introducing well-being programs, physical activities, and psychological support. This recognition is an important signal for companies operating in the border area between Italy and Switzerland, where collaboration between public and private entities is fundamental.

The awarded companies come from various sectors, including manufacturing, services, and commerce. The award ceremony took place in Varese, with the participation of representatives from local institutions and the awarded companies. This event represents an important opportunity to exchange experiences and best practices among the participating companies.

### The Importance of Health at Work

Promoting health in the workplace is fundamental to improving productivity and the well-being of employees. Companies that adhere to the WHP program not only benefit from a healthier work environment but also contribute to creating a positive corporate culture. This is particularly relevant for cross-border workers who work in Ticino and reside in Italy, where the quality of life and work is a priority.

The awarded companies have implemented a series of initiatives, including fitness programs, health training courses, and psychological support services. These initiatives not only improve the quality of life for employees but also contribute to reducing absenteeism and increasing job satisfaction.

### The Awarded Companies

Among the awarded companies are some of the most important economic realities in the area, operating in key sectors such as industry, commerce, and services. The complete list of awarded companies is available on the website of Confindustria Varese and ATS Insubria. This recognition is an important milestone for companies that have demonstrated a concrete commitment to promoting the health and well-being of their employees.

The awarded companies have received a recognition certificate and will be included in a public list of virtuous companies. This recognition is an important incentive for other companies to follow their example and adhere to the WHP program.

### Impact on the Territory

The initiative has a significant impact on the territory, promoting a culture of health and well-being that involves both companies and employees. This is particularly relevant for cross-border workers in Ticino, where the quality of life and work is a priority. The awarded companies represent a positive model for other economic realities in the area, demonstrating that it is possible to reconcile productivity and employee well-being.

The WHP program is an example of how collaboration between public and private entities can lead to concrete results for the well-being of workers. This recognition is an important step forward in promoting a culture of health and well-being in the workplace, contributing to creating a healthier and more productive work environment.

### Conclusions

The awarding of 90 companies in the area by ATS Insubria and Confindustria Varese represents an important recognition for companies that promote health and well-being in the workplace. This program is an example of how collaboration between public and private entities can lead to concrete results for the well-being of workers, particularly for cross-border workers operating in the Canton of Ticino.

The awarded companies represent a positive model for other economic realities in the area, demonstrating that it is possible to reconcile productivity and employee well-being. This recognition is an important incentive for other companies to follow their example and adhere to the WHP program, contributing to creating a healthier and more productive work environment. ## Implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers who commute to Ticino and reside in Italy, joining the WHP program represents an important opportunity to improve their quality of life and work. Companies that join the program offer a series of benefits to their employees, including fitness programs, health training courses, and psychological support services.

### Benefits for employees

Employees of companies that join the WHP program benefit from a series of initiatives aimed at improving their health and well-being. These programs include:

- Fitness programs and physical activities
- Health and well-being training courses
- Psychological support services
- Initiatives for stress prevention and management
- Healthy eating and nutrition programs

These benefits not only improve the quality of life for employees but also help reduce absenteeism and increase job satisfaction. For cross-border workers commuting to Ticino, these programs represent an important opportunity to improve their quality of life and work.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the introduction of the WHP program, many companies did not have health and well-being policies for their employees. This led to a series of problems, including increased absenteeism, reduced productivity, and greater job dissatisfaction. The introduction of the WHP program represented a significant step forward in improving the quality of life and work for employees.

Companies that have joined the WHP program have demonstrated a concrete commitment to promoting the health and well-being of their employees. This has led to a series of benefits, including reduced absenteeism, increased productivity, and greater job satisfaction. For cross-border workers commuting to Ticino, this represents an important opportunity to improve their quality of life and work.

### Future scenarios

Joining the WHP program represents an important opportunity for companies operating in the border area between Italy and Switzerland. Companies that join the program benefit from a series of advantages, including improved quality of life and work for their employees, reduced absenteeism, and increased productivity.

For cross-border workers commuting to Ticino, joining the WHP program represents an important opportunity to improve their quality of life and work. Companies that join the program offer a series of benefits to their employees, including fitness programs, health training courses, and psychological support services. This represents an important opportunity for cross-border workers to improve their quality of life and work.

### Conclusion

Joining the WHP program represents an important opportunity for companies operating in the border area between Italy and Switzerland. Companies that join the program benefit from a series of advantages, including improved quality of life and work for their employees, reduced absenteeism, and increased productivity. For cross-border workers commuting to Ticino, joining the WHP program represents an important opportunity to improve their quality of life and work.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What to do to join the WHP program

To join the WHP program, companies must follow a series of steps. These steps include submitting an application, evaluating the health and well-being initiatives implemented, and participating in a certification process.

### Steps to join

1. Submit the application
2. Evaluation of the health and well-being initiatives implemented
3. Participation in the certification process
4. Recognition and award

Companies wishing to join the WHP program must submit an application to the Confindustria Varese or ATS Insubria website. The application must include a description of the health and well-being initiatives implemented by the company.

### Evaluation of initiatives

The health and well-being initiatives implemented by the company will be evaluated by a commission of experts. The commission will evaluate the quality and effectiveness of the initiatives implemented, as well as their impact on the health and well-being of employees. Companies that achieve a sufficiently high score will be invited to participate in the certification process.

### Certification process

Companies participating in the certification process must demonstrate that they have implemented a series of health and well-being initiatives for their employees. These initiatives must be consistent with the criteria of the WHP program and must be positively evaluated by the commission of experts. Companies that obtain certification will be awarded during an official ceremony.

### Recognition and award

Companies that obtain WHP certification will be awarded during an official ceremony. Awarded companies will receive a certificate of recognition and will be included in a public list of virtuous companies. This recognition is an important incentive for other companies to follow their example and join the WHP program.

### Useful tools

For more information on the WHP program and to submit an application, companies can visit the Confindustria Varese or ATS Insubria website. These sites contain detailed information about the program, evaluation criteria, and steps to join.

In addition, companies can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to evaluate the impact of health and well-being initiatives on their employees. This tool can help companies plan and implement effective and sustainable health and well-being initiatives.

### Conclusion

Joining the WHP program represents an important opportunity for companies operating in the border area between Italy and Switzerland. Companies that join the program benefit from a series of advantages, including an improvement in the quality of life and work of their employees, a reduction in absenteeism, and an increase in productivity. For cross-border workers in Ticino, joining the WHP program represents an important opportunity to improve their quality of life and work.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/21/leggi-notizia/argomenti/economia-13/articolo/promuovere-la-salute-nei-luoghi-di-lavoro-ats-insubria-e-confindustria-varese-premiano-quasi-90-az.html)*

item.title: BancaStato Walking Mendrisio 2026: three routes for everyone
item.description: On Sunday, April 26, 2026, in Mendrisio, three routes of varying difficulty, a free lunch, and entertainment for the new edition of BancaStato Walking Mendrisio.
item.content:encoded:
On Sunday, April 26, 2026, the BancaStato Walking Mendrisio event returns, taking place at the Centro Manifestazioni Mercato Coperto in Mendrisio. The 2026 edition offers three routes of varying difficulty: Family (3.6 km), Castle (9.2 km), and Salorino (12.1 km). The Family route is flat and accessible to everyone, including those with strollers. The Castle route, of medium difficulty, winds through the countryside and vineyards of the municipalities of Castello S. Pietro and Salorino. The Salorino route, the most challenging, offers a spectacular panorama and passes through Corteglia, Castel S. Pietro, Obino, Salorino, and Somazzo.

### 📊 Route Details

- **Family (3.6 km)**: Ideal for families and beginners, this route is completely flat and passes through the center of Mendrisio, the Parco delle Valli, and Lake Mendrisio. The average time to complete the route is about 45 minutes.
- Castle (9.2 km): This medium-difficulty route has an elevation gain of 200 meters and offers breathtaking views of the vineyards and surrounding hills. The average time to complete the route is about 2 hours and 30 minutes.
- Salorino (12.1 km): The most challenging route, with an elevation gain of 350 meters, passes through the municipalities of Mendrisio, Castel S. Pietro, Obino, Salorino, and Somazzo. The average time to complete the route is about 3 hours and 30 minutes.

### 🐕 Walk&Dog: The Category for Animal Lovers

For animal lovers, the Walk&Dog category is available on all routes, which includes a bib with the owner's and dog's name and a commemorative bowl. Registrations are open online at www.walkingmendrisio.ch until April 22 or on-site on Saturday, April 25 (2:30 PM - 5:00 PM) and Sunday, April 26 (from 8:00 AM). The registration fee is CHF 20 for adults and CHF 10 for children under 12. For the Walk&Dog category, there is an additional fee of CHF 5.

### ⚠️ Regulations and Operational Checklist

- Registrations: Online registrations close on April 22. It is possible to register on-site, but it is advisable to do so in advance to avoid queues.
- Documents: Bring a valid ID and, if participating in the Walk&Dog category, the dog's microchip.
- Clothing: Dress appropriately for the chosen route. For the Salorino route, it is recommended to wear hiking shoes and bring a walking stick.
- Hydration: Bring a water bottle. Along the routes, there will be refreshment points, but it is always better to be prepared.

### 💡 What to Expect at the Event

At the Centro Manifestazioni Mercato Coperto in Mendrisio, the event village will be set up with stands, exhibitors, and a dining area where lunch will be offered to all participants. Before each start, a warm-up is planned to prepare for the walk.

> "Last year we had over 1,000 participants and we expect a similar number this year," said the event organizer.

### 📌 Practical Scenario Comparisons

- Family Route vs. Castle Route: The Family route is ideal for a relaxing walk, while the Castle route offers a slightly more challenging experience with an elevation gain of 200 meters.
- Castle Route vs. Salorino Route: The Salorino route is the most challenging, with an elevation gain of 350 meters and a length of 12.1 km. It is recommended only for those with good physical preparation.

### 📍 Useful Information

- Parking: Free parking is available at the Centro Manifestazioni Mercato Coperto in Mendrisio.
- Public Transport: It is possible to reach Mendrisio by train or bus. Mendrisio station is a few minutes' walk from the event center.
- Accommodation: For those coming from outside, there are various accommodation options in Mendrisio and nearby municipalities such as Morcote, Lugano, and Chiasso.

Don't miss this opportunity to enjoy the nature and landscape of Ticino with friends and family! The BancaStato Walking Mendrisio 2026 event is a unique opportunity to spend a day outdoors, surrounded by the beauty of the Ticino landscape. The routes have been designed to offer different experiences, suitable for all levels of physical preparation. The Family route, approximately 5 km long, is ideal for those who want to take a relaxing walk, with a mere 150 meters of elevation gain. The Salorino route, which winds for 12 km with a 400-meter elevation gain, is perfect for those seeking a more challenging experience, crossing the picturesque forests of Balerna and Salorino. The Castello route, with its 8 km and a 250-meter elevation gain, represents a good compromise for those who want to enjoy the landscape without overexerting themselves, passing near the Castello di Mendrisio.

### Walk&Dog: a unique experience for animal lovers

The Walk&Dog category is a novelty that allows participants to bring their four-legged friends with them. This route is valid on all trails and includes a bib with the owner's and dog's names and a commemorative bowl. Registrations for this category are open until April 22, and the cost is 20 CHF for adults and 10 CHF for dogs. It is important to remember that dogs must be kept on a leash and have a microchip, as required by cantonal regulations.

### How to participate

To participate in the event, you can register online at www.walkingmendrisio.ch by April 22 or on-site on Saturday, April 25 (2:30 PM - 5:00 PM) and Sunday, April 26 (from 8:00 AM). The event is open to everyone and represents an opportunity to spend a day in good company, focused on sports and fun. The registration fee is 30 CHF for adults and 15 CHF for young people under 16 years old. You can choose from different registration options, including the possibility to participate in multiple routes on the same day.

### Operational checklist

Before participating, it is useful to prepare an operational checklist:
- Check the chosen route and level of difficulty.
- Verify the necessary equipment: comfortable shoes, suitable clothing, water, and snacks.
- Ensure you have your bib and commemorative bowl if participating in the Walk&Dog category.
- **Check** the weather conditions and prepare accordingly.

### Comparison of practical scenarios

For those seeking a physical challenge, the Salorino route offers a more intense experience, with greater elevation gain and length compared to the Family route. Those who prefer a more relaxed experience can opt for the Castello route, which offers a good balance between difficulty and scenic beauty. The Walk&Dog category is ideal for those who want to combine physical activity with the pleasure of spending time with their dog.

### Regulations and important dates

It is important to remember that the event takes place in compliance with cantonal regulations regarding safety and animal welfare. Registrations close on April 22, and the event will take place on April 25 and 26. For further information, you can consult the official website or contact the organization.

> "The BancaStato Walking Mendrisio event is an opportunity to discover the beauty of Ticino in an active and fun way."

The BancaStato Walking Mendrisio 2026 event is a must for those who love nature and sports, offering routes suitable for all needs and the possibility of participating with their dog. Don't miss the opportunity to live a unique experience in the heart of Ticino.

## Useful tools for your case
To verify your within/over 20 km tax scenario, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [tax return guide](nav:tax-return). To participate in BancaStato Walking Mendrisio 2026, follow these steps:

1. **Register online**: Visit the website www.walkingmendrisio.ch and complete the registration process by April 22. The online registration fee is CHF 25 until March 31, and CHF 30 from April 1 to April 22. Payments can be made by credit card or PayPal.

2. Register on-site: If you prefer, you can register on Saturday, April 25, from 14.30 to 17.00 at the Mendrisio Exhibition Center or on Sunday, April 26, from 8.00 at the start of the routes. The on-site registration fee is CHF 35.

3. Choose your route: Choose from the three available routes based on your physical preparation and interests. The 10 km 'Panorama' route passes through the municipalities of Mendrisio, Morbio Inferiore, and Coldrerio, offering breathtaking views of the Alps. The 15 km 'Adventure' route includes a section through the Balerna forest and an ascent to Monte San Giorgio, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. The 20 km 'Exploration' route passes through the municipalities of Mendrisio, Novazzano, Chiasso, and Morcote, with a stretch along Lake Lugano.

4. Join the warm-up: Before each start, join the warm-up to prepare for the walk. The warm-ups are led by qualified instructors and take place 30 minutes before each start.

5. Enjoy the event: Participate in the walk, visit the stands and exhibitors, and enjoy the lunch provided. The lunch includes traditional Ticino dishes such as cheese risotto, polenta, and bresaola, as well as vegetarian and vegan options.

### Use our calculator to plan your participation

To help you plan your participation, use our [calculator](nav:calculator) to estimate the time needed to complete your chosen route. Enter your average walking speed and the route distance to get an accurate estimate of the completion time. For example, if you walk at an average speed of 5 km/h, the 10 km 'Panorama' route will take you about 2 hours, while the 20 km 'Exploration' route will take you about 4 hours.

### Compare the routes

Use our comparator to compare the three available routes and choose the one that best suits your needs. Compare the distance, difficulty, and views offered by each route to make the best choice. For example, the 'Panorama' route is ideal for those looking for a relaxing walk with panoramic views, while the 'Adventure' route is perfect for those who love more challenging routes with forest sections and climbs.

### Operational checklist

- Documents: Bring a valid ID and your registration confirmation.
- Clothing: Wear comfortable clothing and hiking shoes.
- Hydration: Bring a water bottle to stay hydrated during the walk.
- Sun protection: Apply sunscreen and bring sunglasses.
- Food: Bring energy snacks to keep your energy up during the walk.

### Regulations and rules

- Insurance: All participants are covered by accident insurance during the event.
- Safety: Always follow the instructions of the organizers and volunteers.
- Environment: Respect the environment and do not leave litter along the route.

### Practical Scenario Comparisons

- Scenario 1: If you have limited time and want to enjoy a panoramic view, choose the 'Panorama' route of 10 km.
- Scenario 2: If you are an experienced walker and want a more challenging experience, opt for the 'Adventure' route of 15 km.
- Scenario 3: If you want a complete experience that includes various landscapes and towns, choose the 'Exploration' route of 20 km.

> "The BancaStato Walking Mendrisio is an event that combines sports, nature, and community. I can't wait to participate this year!" - Participant of the 2025 edition.

### Useful Tips

- Booking: Register as soon as possible to secure your spot and take advantage of the reduced rate.
- Accommodation: If you are coming from outside the region, book your accommodation in advance to find the best offers.
- Transport: Use public transport to reach the starting point. Public transport services are well connected to Mendrisio and neighboring towns.

With these steps and tools, you will be ready to participate in the BancaStato Walking Mendrisio 2026 and enjoy this unique experience in the heart of Ticino to the fullest.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/domenica-26-aprile-ce-bancastato-walking-mendrisiotto-431888)*

item.title: Company hires drivers in Lombardy: 800 euros per month, then contract and 3000 euro bonus
item.description: FNM Autoservizi is looking for new drivers with a paid training program and a 3000 euro sign-on bonus
item.content:encoded:
## Opportunities for New Drivers in Lombardy

FNM Autoservizi has opened selections for the new edition of the Academy, a training program aimed at people aged 21 and over. The initiative includes a fixed-term part-time employment contract for the entire duration of the training period aimed at obtaining a D license and CQC, with all costs covered by the company. At the end of the course, new drivers will be hired on a full-time permanent basis and will receive a sign-on bonus of 3,000 euros.

### The Training Program

The training program will take place at the Saronno and Tradate locations and includes an initial fixed-term part-time employment contract as an Operations Assistant. During this period, candidates will obtain the D license and CQC, with all costs covered by the company. The expected salary is approximately 800 euros per month. At the end of the training period, those who have successfully passed the exams will be offered a permanent contract.

### Selection Requirements

The selection is open to all individuals aged 21 and over, with a lower secondary school diploma, a B license, physical and psycho-attitudinal fitness for the job, a clean criminal record, and no pending charges. Additionally, candidates must be available to work shifts, have a passion for driving, and possess good interpersonal skills.

## Implications for Cross-Border Workers

This opportunity could also be of interest to cross-border workers residing in Ticino and working in Lombardy. The training program offered by FNM Autoservizi represents a concrete possibility to acquire new skills and improve their professional position. Furthermore, the sign-on bonus of 3,000 euros is a significant incentive for those who decide to pursue this career.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before this initiative, cross-border workers who wanted to work as drivers in Lombardy often had to cover the costs of training and licensing on their own. Additionally, opportunities for permanent employment were less frequent. With this new Academy, FNM Autoservizi offers a structured and paid training path, reducing costs and increasing job stability.

## Concrete Actions for Candidates

To apply for the selection, you can fill out the form in the online announcement at the following link: [https://joblink.allibo.com/ats3/job-offer.aspx?DM=1954&ID=103927&LN=IT&FT=554&SG=2](https://joblink.allibo.com/ats3/job-offer.aspx?DM=1954&ID=103927&LN=IT&FT=554&SG=2).
